- **Protobuf now indexes `enum` as `enum`, and captures `package`, `oneof`, and `extend` declarations (#172)** — `SymbolExtractor` now re-kinds protobuf `enum` declarations correctly, records `package` lines as `namespace` symbols, and surfaces `oneof` / `extend` blocks so protobuf schemas show up in `symbols`, `definition`, and `outline` with the right structural shape. Added a focused regression fixture that covers all four cases and preserves `service` / `rpc` extraction.
- **Terraform symbol extraction now covers core configuration blocks (#174)** - `locals` blocks now keep a stable `locals` name instead of the literal `locals {` text, and `provider`, `terraform`, `import`, `moved`, `removed`, and `check` blocks are indexed as symbols.
- **Lua symbol extraction now recognizes assignment-form function definitions (#192)** — `SymbolExtractor` now captures `local name = function(...)` and table-member assignment forms such as `M.name = function(...)` / `M:name = function(...)` in addition to the existing `function name(...)` and `local function name(...)` patterns. The Lua regression test now covers the new shapes alongside the existing `require` and plain function cases, and the Lua reference row in `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md` reflects the broader coverage.
- **F# `let` bindings now skip modifier keywords and keep quoted identifiers (#207)** — the F# extractor now recognizes `let mutable`, `let inline`, `let private`, `let internal`, and `let public` bindings by their real names instead of indexing the modifier keyword, still captures plain `let x = ...` bindings, and normalizes backtick-quoted identifiers like ``let ``spaced name`` = ...`` to `spaced name`.
- **Rust macro invocations now appear in `references`, `callers`, `callees`, and `impact` (#258)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now recognizes Rust macro call syntax with `!` plus `()`, `[]`, or `{}`, including path-qualified forms like `std::println!`, and it suppresses the `macro_rules!` declaration keyword so macro definitions do not double-count as calls.
- **Go generic type declarations now appear in `symbols` again (#262)** — `SymbolExtractor` now accepts optional Go type-parameter blocks between the type name and `struct` / `interface` / type-alias bodies, so `type Stack[T any] struct { ... }` and similar declarations no longer disappear from the index. Added a regression covering generic structs, interfaces, and defined types. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Fixes #262.
- **Rust turbofish call sites now keep their method references (#271)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now accepts an optional `::` between a call name and its generic arguments, so Rust forms like `value.parse::<i32>()` continue to emit `call` rows instead of silently dropping the reference. Added a regression test for a turbofish method call alongside a plain non-turbofish baseline, and documented the Rust language row in `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Fixes #271.
- **Rust constructor calls now survive the shared call-name ignore list (#273)** — `Type::new(...)` and `Default::default()` are now preserved for Rust while the shared `new` / `default` suppression still applies to other languages. Added regression coverage for Rust constructors and the Go `new(T)` builtin.
- **Scala block-call forms now emit call edges (#277)** — `foreach { ... }`, `Try { ... }`, `synchronized { ... }`, and similar `name { ... }` sites are indexed again, so Scala call-graph queries no longer miss idiomatic block-call usage.
- **Ruby block-call forms now appear in `references`, `callers`, `callees`, and `impact` (#278)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now recognizes idiomatic Ruby block-call syntax such as `items.each { ... }`, `items.each_with_index do ... end`, and `with_transaction do ... end`, while keeping hash literals from being mistaken for calls.
- **Elixir symbol bodies now close at the matching `end` instead of the first nested one (#279)** — `SymbolExtractor` now tracks Elixir `do ... end` / `fn ... end` nesting separately from Ruby, so functions that contain nested `fn`, `case`, `if`, or `with` blocks keep the full body range. Added regressions for nested blocks, `, do:` shorthand, and downstream reference container attribution.
- **Gradle block DSL and command-style calls now produce reference rows (#280)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now captures idiomatic Gradle/Groovy forms such as `plugins { ... }`, `dependencies { ... }`, `task buildJar(type: Jar) { ... }`, `apply plugin: 'java'`, `implementation '...'`, and `println 'x'` instead of dropping them behind the shared paren-only matcher and global keyword filter.
- **Terraform dotted references now index as `reference` edges (#285)** — `var.*`, `local.*`, `module.*`, `data.*`, and same-file resource-like `TYPE.NAME` references such as `aws_instance.web` and `depends_on = [aws_s3_bucket.foo]` are now visible to `references`, `callers`, and `callees`.
- **Vue and Svelte SFC script blocks now index as TypeScript/JavaScript** — `.vue` and `.svelte` files now route their `<script>` content through the existing TS/JS symbol and reference extractors, so `symbols`, `definition`, `outline`, `callers`, `callees`, and `map` no longer come back empty for component scripts.
- **Haskell `instance` declarations no longer emit phantom `class` symbols (#311)** - the Haskell symbol extractor now limits the `class` row to `data`, `newtype`, and `type` declarations, so `instance Greeter Person where` no longer duplicates the real `class Greeter a where` symbol.
- **Dart constructors and `typedef` aliases are now indexed (#312)** — `cdidx` now captures default, named, and factory constructor declarations, along with `const` constructors that use `this` / `super` initializers, plus both modern and legacy `typedef` aliases, so Dart symbol queries no longer drop most of a class's public API surface.
- **Ruby `attr_accessor` now captures every name in comma-separated lists, and `alias` / `alias_method` declarations are indexed** — `cdidx definition` can now resolve each name in `attr_accessor :a, :b, :c` and similar `attr_reader` / `attr_writer` forms, and Ruby alias declarations now produce function symbols for the introduced name.
- **Go `const` and `var` declarations are now indexed as `property` symbols, and top-level `const` rows are no longer skipped (#314)** — the Go extractor now recognizes package-level `const` declarations at column 0, keeps const-block entries, and maps both `const` and `var` rows to the `property` kind so `symbols` and `definition` return the expected names.
- **PHP enum cases are now indexed as symbols (#318)** — `case Pending;` and backed cases such as `case USD = 'USD';` are now captured inside PHP enums, with backed values preserved in `return_type` so `symbols`, `definition`, and `outline` can surface the full enum value set.
- **Swift enum cases and `indirect enum` declarations are now indexed (#319)** — `SymbolExtractor` now accepts `indirect enum` declarations and captures enum cases such as `case timeout`, `case server(code: Int, message: String)`, `case inactive = "off"`, and `indirect case node(...)` as `property` symbols, while keeping switch-arm `case ...:` lines out of the index. Added regressions for raw-value cases, associated-value cases, `indirect case`, `indirect enum`, and switch-case false positives. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Fixes #319.
- **Rust enum variants are now indexed as symbols (#321)** - `Ok(T)`, `Err(E)`, struct-like variants such as `Circle { radius: f64 }`, and bare variants like `Point` now appear in `symbols`, `definition`, and related queries.
- **Scala nested interpolator-prefixed triple strings inside holes keep inner calls visible (#996)** — nested `s"""...${innerCall()}..."""`-style literals opened inside a Scala `${...}` hole continue to preserve real call references inside their own interpolation holes while still masking the surrounding body.

#### Internal

- **Broaden the `git clean` rule example to stay representative** — the rule now uses `git clean -fdx` as the example command and matches the broader `git clean` prefix, addressing the follow-up candidate from PR #1014.

### [1.17.0] - 2026-04-30

#### Changed
- **Ordinary changelog updates now flow through `changelog.d/unreleased/` fragments** — ordinary implementation PRs no longer edit `CHANGELOG.md` directly; release preparation aggregates bilingual fragments, preserves any legacy unreleased content, and updates the compare footer. Affected: `.codex/workflows/changelog-fragment.md`, `.codex/workflows/release-changelog.md`, `AGENT_GUIDE.md`, `changelog.d/README.md`, `tools/CodeIndex.Changelog/Program.cs`.

#### Fixed
- **Body-less C# brace members now stop at `;` even with trailing comments (#245)** — `FindBraceRange` now treats a semicolon as the terminator before any trailing `//` or `/* ... */` comment on the declaration line, so interface and abstract members no longer spill into the next sibling body. Added a regression for the `int D(int y); /* trailing block comment */` shape and the follow-on class body. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Fixes #245.
- **Ruby command-syntax calls and common DSL targets now appear in `references` / `callers` (#260)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now recognizes Ruby no-parens call forms such as `puts "hi"`, `greet bob`, `require 'json'`, `include Shared`, `attr_accessor :name`, `before_action :authenticate`, and `raise ArgumentError, "bad"`, so the idiomatic command syntax no longer disappears from the graph. Added Ruby regressions for no-parens calls, mixin targets, attribute targets, callback targets, and `raise` exception-class targets. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `README.md`. Fixes #260.
- **Java generic methods now index correctly, and switch expressions no longer emit phantom `function switch` / `function <TypeName>` rows (#225)** — `SymbolExtractor` now accepts Java type-parameter prefixes such as `public <T> T first(...)` / `public <K, V> V get(...)` and blocks statement keywords like `return` and `case` from being misread as method signatures. Added regression coverage for generic methods and Java 21 switch expressions with pattern arms. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Fixes #225.
- **Rust `impl Trait for Type` blocks now attach to the implementing type, and `unsafe impl` is indexed (#169)** — `SymbolExtractor` now distinguishes trait impls from inherent impls so `impl Debug for Widget` / `impl<T> Future for Task<T>` are attributed to `Widget` / `Task` instead of fake trait-named classes, and `unsafe impl` blocks are no longer dropped. Added regression coverage for trait, unsafe, and inherent impl forms. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Fixes #169.
- **`--path` and `--exclude-path` now understand glob-style wildcards (#195)** — Path filters still treat plain strings as substring matches, but `*` and `?` are translated to SQL LIKE wildcards so commands like `find --path "*.py"` and `find --path "src/*.py"` work as documented. Added regression coverage for the glob translation and `find` output, and updated CLI/help/docs/MCP wording. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Cli/ConsoleUi.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Cli/QueryCommandRunner.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Mcp/McpToolDefinitions.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/DbReaderTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `README.md`. Fixes #195.
- **`--exclude-tests` no longer drops mid-word filenames like `latest.py` and `request.py` (#188)** — Tightened the shared test-path heuristic so it only matches common test path boundaries such as `tests/`, `test_*.py`, `*_test.py`, and `__tests__/`. Mid-word filenames like `latest.py`, `fastest.py`, `contest.py`, `request.py`, `ingest.rs`, and `digest.go` now stay in non-test query results. Added regression coverage for both `symbols` and `search`. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/DbReaderTests.cs`, `README.md`. Fixes #188.
- **Python now indexes PEP 695 generic functions and type aliases (#197)** — `SymbolExtractor` now accepts `def name[T](...)` / `async def name[T](...)` and `type Name = ...` / `type Name[T] = ...` in Python 3.12 source, so modern generic declarations show up in `symbols`, `definition`, and `outline` again. Type aliases surface as `import` symbols, matching the existing alias convention used by other languages. Added regressions for generic functions, class-body aliases, and soft-keyword negative controls. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`, `README.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Fixes #197.
- **Python bare decorators like `@staticmethod` and `@pytest.fixture` are now captured as `decorator` references (#259)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now records bare Python decorators without call parentheses, so `references`, `callers`, and `impact` can see `@decorator`, `@staticmethod`, and qualified forms like `@pytest.fixture` instead of dropping them silently. Added regression coverage for bare, stacked, and qualified decorator forms. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Fixes #259.
- **`backfill-fold --json` now stays trim-safe instead of aborting on anonymous JSON output (#181)** — `RunBackfillFold` now serializes its success and error envelopes through source-generated DTOs, so the published trimmed release no longer throws `InvalidOperationException` / SIGABRT when `--json` is used. Added a regression that publishes a trimmed `cdidx.dll` and exercises both the success and missing-db JSON paths. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Cli/IndexCommandRunner.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Cli/JsonOutputContracts.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/IndexCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Fixes #181.
- **C/C++ out-of-class member definitions, concepts, inline namespaces, and modules are now indexed (#209)** — `SymbolExtractor` now captures qualified member definitions such as `Foo::bar()`, constructors and destructors, conversion and assignment operators, `template <> int add<int>(...)` specializations, `int Foo::counter = 0;` static member definitions, `concept` declarations, `inline namespace` blocks, and `export module` declarations so the graph tools can see normal C++ impl files again. Added a regression fixture that exercises the core shapes together. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Fixes #209.
- **Dockerfile stage dependencies now show up in `callers` and `impact` (#275)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now records Dockerfile `FROM <stage> AS <new>` and `COPY --from=<stage>` edges as `call` references when the source name matches a named stage in the same file, so intermediate build stages no longer look unused. Added regression coverage for stage-to-stage `FROM`, `COPY --from`, and the base-image negative control. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`, `CLAUDE.md`. Fixes #275.
- **`unused` now recognizes class-level reflection attributes and common C# property attributes (#182)** — `DbReader` now promotes public class/struct/interface/enum symbols with nearby reflection/config attributes such as `[Serializable]`, `[JsonSerializable]`, `[Table]`, and `[AttributeUsage]`, and it also recognizes property attributes like `[Key]`, `[Required]`, `[BindProperty]`, `[Parameter]`, `[Inject]`, `[Obsolete]`, and `[BindNever]`. Added regressions for type-level and property-level fixtures. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbSymbolReader.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/DbReaderTests.cs`, `README.md`. Fixes #182.
- **Makefile variable assignments now stay out of `function` symbols, and `%` pattern rules are indexed (#167)** — `:=` / `::=` / `=` / `?=` / `+=` assignments are now classified as `property` symbols instead of fake Makefile targets, while `%` pattern rules such as `%.o: %.c` are now captured as real `function` targets. Added regression coverage for assignment forms, pattern rules, and the existing target forms. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Fixes #167.
- **C / C++ `#define` macros now index as symbols, and define lines no longer emit phantom `call` edges (#267)** — `SymbolExtractor` now captures both function-like and object-like `#define` macros in C and C++, so macro names such as `MAX`, `VERSION`, and `MAX_BUFFER` show up in `symbols`, `definition`, and `outline`. `ReferenceExtractor` now suppresses the define-line self-match once the macro symbol exists, so `#define MAX(a, b)` no longer produces a fake `call MAX` row while real use sites still do. Added C and C++ regressions for symbol extraction and define-line suppression. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Fixes #267.
- **MCP notifications now stay silent instead of returning `id: null` errors (#186)** — `McpServer` now short-circuits every JSON-RPC notification before any error envelope is emitted, so unknown `notifications/*` messages no longer get a `-32601 Method not found` response with `id: null`. Known handshake notifications still stay silent, and unknown notifications are logged to stderr for observability. Added a regression test for the silent stdout path and stderr log. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Mcp/McpServer.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/McpServerTests.cs`. Fixes #186.
- **MCP JSON-RPC responses now preserve explicit `id: null` and reject boolean ids (#204)** — The MCP server now distinguishes a missing `id` member from an explicit `null`, so requests such as `{"id": null, ...}` keep `id: null` in success and error responses instead of silently dropping the field. Requests with invalid boolean ids now return `-32600` with `id: null` instead of echoing the boolean back. Added regression coverage for both the null-id and boolean-id cases. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Mcp/McpServer.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/McpServerTests.cs`. Fixes #204.
- **File detection now recognizes OCaml, Erlang, Clojure, Nim, Crystal, Julia, Perl, Tcl, Solidity, and D extensions (#202)** — `FileIndexer` now maps those extensions into their language buckets, so projects in those languages are no longer silently indexed as zero files. Added regression coverage for `GetLanguageExtensions` and the `languages` CLI JSON output, and updated the language tables in `README.md` and `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md` to match. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/FileIndexer.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/FileIndexerTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `README.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Fixes #202.
- **Kotlin / Scala expression-bodied functions now keep single-line ranges (#210)** — `SymbolExtractor` now recognizes single-line `fun foo() = expr` / `def foo() = expr` declarations and stops their end lines at the declaration line instead of scanning forward to an unrelated later closing brace. Added Kotlin and Scala regressions that interleave expression-bodied and block-bodied functions. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Fixes #210.
- **MCP `tools/list` and structured `tools/call` now return JSON-RPC errors instead of silently disappearing on unexpected serialization failures (#178)** — `McpServer` now configures a trim-safe `JsonSerializerOptions` resolver for MCP responses and emits a JSON-RPC `-32603` error envelope when a request handler or response serialization throws, so AI clients no longer hang on the first MCP handshake step. Added a regression that forces response serialization to fail and asserts the server still returns an error response with the original request id. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Mcp/McpServer.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/McpServerTests.cs`. Fixes #178.
- **PR creation now includes a Markdown sanity check for the body** — The PR finalization workflow now requires agents to draft the description as real multiline Markdown, re-read it before creation, and reject literal `\n` / `\t` / `\r` escape text outside code fences so malformed PR bodies do not get published. Affected: `.codex/workflows/pr-finalize.md`.
- **Groovy, assembly, CUDA, shader, HDL, Lisp, Pascal, Ada, Fortran, Raku, and Perl test extensions now index instead of being silently dropped (#205)** — Added `LangMap` coverage for the missing mainstream extensions so `cdidx languages`, `files`, and `search` can see them instead of producing an empty index on those repositories. Added regression coverage for direct detection and scan-time indexing. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/FileIndexer.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/FileIndexerTests.cs`, `README.md`. Fixes #205.
- **CSS selector lists now keep every selector, ID selectors use the class kind, and named at-rules are indexed (#235)** — `SymbolExtractor` now expands comma-separated CSS selector lists on the same line, classifies `#id` selectors with the same `class` kind as `.class`, and adds coverage for `@counter-style`, `@page`, `@namespace`, and semicolon-form `@layer` names. Added regressions for selector lists, named at-rules, and the query/definition contract. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Fixes #235.
- **TypeScript interface member properties and abstract class properties now index as `property` symbols (#218)** — Added a `property` extraction row so semicolon-terminated members such as `id: string;`, `readonly age: number;`, `timeout?: number;`, and `abstract readonly count: number;` are visible again inside TypeScript interface and abstract class bodies. Existing method and typed-arrow extraction paths stay unchanged. Added regression coverage in `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Fixes #218.
- **PowerShell class members and enum values now index alongside the shell declarations (#216)** — `SymbolExtractor` now captures PowerShell class constructors, return-typed methods, properties, `static` / `hidden` members, and enum values, so `symbols`, `definition`, and `outline` no longer collapse PS5+ classes down to only the outer `class` / `enum` shell. Added a regression covering a class with constructor, method, property, static, and hidden members plus enum members. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`, `README.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Fixes #216.
- **Claude entry point now points at the current status trust contract** — `CLAUDE.md` now mirrors the `status --json` trust fields documented in `README.md` and `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`, including the SQL graph readiness and degradation fields plus the fold-specific remediation fields, so the agent entry point stays synchronized with the shipped payload contract.
- **Type aliases now index as `import` symbols across Rust, TypeScript, Swift, Go, F#, and Scala (#206)** — `SymbolExtractor` now maps alias-like declarations in those languages to `import` instead of `class`, adds a dedicated Scala `type ... = ...` rule, and splits F# record / union / constructor-style `type` declarations into `struct` / `enum` / `class` so the shared language table matches the actual symbol kinds. Added regressions for each affected language. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Fixes #206.
- **Method references and callback handoffs now stay visible in the graph (#239)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now emits handoff edges for Java / Kotlin / Scala `::` references and C# method groups such as `Action a = Fire;` / `new Func<int, int>(Compute);`, so functional-style references no longer disappear just because they omit `(`. Added regressions for Java and C# handoff forms. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Fixes #239.
- **C# same-line definition suppression now preserves recursive and delegated calls (#252)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now suppresses only the first same-line call-site match for each definition name, so expression-bodied recursion and one-line delegating wrappers keep their later `call` edges instead of disappearing with the declaration token. Added a regression that covers `Fib(...)`, `FibBlock(...)`, and `log.DoLog()` on the same line as their definitions. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`. Fixes #252.
- **`impact --depth 0` now resolves the symbol without traversing callers (#193)** — `DbReader.AnalyzeImpact` now short-circuits at depth 0 and returns the resolved definitions with an empty caller list instead of silently falling through to the default traversal depth. The CLI `impact` command now passes the parsed depth through unchanged, returns success for depth-0 symbol-resolution queries, and prints a specific note that callers were not traversed. MCP `impact_analysis` now accepts `maxDepth: 0` with matching documentation, and the README tables now describe the resolve-only mode. Added regressions for the database reader, CLI JSON output, and MCP tool call. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Cli/QueryCommandRunner.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Cli/ConsoleUi.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Mcp/McpToolHandlers.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Mcp/McpToolDefinitions.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/DbReaderTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/McpServerTests.cs`, `README.md`. Fixes #193.
- **C# multiline verbatim/raw string bodies now stay out of `call` and `instantiate` reference rows (#274)** — Added regression coverage to pin multiline string bodies that contain call-shaped or constructor-shaped text, so `references`, `callers`, and `impact` keep treating them as data rather than code. Affected: `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`. Fixes #274.
- **JavaScript prototype / namespace assignments and named function expressions now index again (#227)** — `SymbolExtractor` now captures qualified assignments such as `Vehicle.prototype.start = function () {}`, `Foo.Bar = class {}`, and `Vehicle.VERSION = "1.0"`, named function expressions such as `var add = function add() {}`, and object-literal `function` / arrow property values such as `onSubmit: function submitHandler() {}` and `onClose: () => {}`. Added regression coverage for the qualified class/function/property assignments, the object-literal property values, and the nested class member container so `definition`, `symbols`, and `outline` keep the outer anchor instead of dropping back to a floating inner name. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Fixes #227.
- **`outline` and `excerpt` now accept absolute file paths with explicit `--db` when the DB exposes `project_root` (#631)** — Query commands now resolve absolute paths under the indexed project root back to the stored relative file path before lookup, so callers do not need to strip the root manually when working from outside the workspace. Added regressions that compare relative and absolute input paths for both commands. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Cli/DbPathResolver.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Cli/QueryCommandRunner.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Fixes #631.
- **JSX / TSX component open tags now index as references (#269)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now scans `.jsx` / `.tsx` files for capitalized JSX open tags, emits both the component binding and leaf segment for dotted tags like `<UI.Card>`, and leaves lowercase intrinsic tags alone. Added regressions for positive JSX capture and the `.ts` negative guard. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Cli/IndexCommandRunner.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Mcp/McpToolHandlers.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Fixes #269.
- **C# impact now preserves the original query string on verbatim misses (#960)** — `impact` keeps the user-entered spelling in `resolved_name` when a C# verbatim lookup misses, and the zero-result human path now echoes the original query instead of a canonicalized variant. Added regressions for direct `DbReader.AnalyzeImpact` and CLI JSON/human output. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Cli/QueryCommandRunner.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/DbReaderTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Fixes #960.
- **SCSS `@function`, `@forward`, and compound selectors now index correctly (#168)** — `SymbolExtractor` now captures SCSS `@function` declarations, recognizes `@forward` as an import, and keeps the first class in compound / descendant selectors like `.btn.primary` and `.alert .link` visible. Added regression coverage for `@function`, `@forward`, and selector variants. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Fixes #168.
- **Swift / Kotlin trailing-lambda call sites now stay visible in the reference graph (#265)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now emits `call` edges for idiomatic `name { ... }` forms such as `items.forEach { }`, `items.filter { ... }`, and Swift multi-trailing-closure calls like `animate { } completion: { }`, so the reference graph no longer drops those call sites just because they omit `(`. Added Swift and Kotlin regressions covering trailing-lambda calls and an inheritance-style negative control. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`. Fixes #265.
- **CSS now indexes `var()`, `animation-name`, shorthand `animation:`, and compound class-selector references (#282)** — `ReferenceExtractor` adds CSS-specific reference passes so custom properties, keyframe names, and selector cross-references show up in `references` and stop being reported as unused. Added a focused regression covering `var(--primary-color)`, `var(--spacing-unit)`, `animation-name: fade-in`, `animation: fade-in 0.3s ease-in`, compound selectors, and nested at-rule selectors. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Fixes #282.
- **SQL qualified graph readers no longer fall back to a bare leaf when the call site can still be qualified (#748)** — `AllowLeafFallbackAtColumn` now treats a reference as qualified once `ResolveReferenceNameAtColumn` can recover a schema-qualified name from the call context or container, so `deps`, `hotspots`, `unused`, and `callers` stop attributing a single `EXEC dbo.fn_Target` site to every same-leaf target across schemas. Added a regression that pins the schema-scoped reader result and the helper-level fallback gate. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SqlNameResolver.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/DbReaderTests.cs`. Fixes #748.
- **PowerShell cmdlet and function calls are now indexed as references (#281)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now uses a PowerShell-specific command-start pass so hyphenated Verb-Noun calls like `Get-ChildItem`, `Write-Host`, and pipeline invocations like `$items | ForEach-Object { ... }` are captured even without parentheses. Added a regression covering user-defined functions, built-in cmdlets, pipeline stages, and a non-call `-lt` guard. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Fixes #281.
- **Kotlin `typealias` declarations now index as import symbols (#297)** — `SymbolExtractor` now captures Kotlin `typealias` declarations, including visibility modifiers and generic parameters, so `definition`, `symbols`, and `outline` no longer drop them. Added regression coverage for plain, visibility-bearing, and generic `typealias` lines, plus a non-`typealias` negative case. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Fixes #297.
- **SCSS `$variable` and `@extend %placeholder` references now index again (#283)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now scans CSS/SCSS lines for paren-less `$variable` usages and SCSS `@extend` selectors, so `references` / `callers` can see common Sass reuse patterns instead of dropping them behind the generic `CallRegex` path. Added a regression that covers `$primary`, `$spacing-base`, `@extend %button-base`, and the `@mixin` parameter negative case. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`, `README.md`. Fixes #283.
- **SQL quoted TVF call sites now stay visible in exact-name `references` queries (#658)** — `references --exact-name` now keeps bracketed and backtick-quoted table-valued function calls such as `FROM [dbo].[fn_GetUserStats](42)` and ``FROM `fn_GetUserStats`(42)`` on the `call` path instead of letting them disappear from `symbol_references`. Added a regression that indexes a temp SQL file and checks the JSON output for the quoted, backtick-quoted, and bare control forms. Affected: `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`.
- **CSS grouping at-rules and native nesting selectors now keep their visible names (#307)** — `SymbolExtractor` now emits `namespace` rows for `@layer` / `@container` / `@supports` / `@media` blocks and recognizes native CSS nesting selectors such as `&:hover`, `& .icon`, `&.modifier`, and `& > .child`, so nested styles stay visible to `symbols`, `definition`, `outline`, and `map`. Added regression coverage for grouped at-rules, nested ampersand selectors, and the existing true-nesting negative control. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Fixes #307.
- **Split-line SQL `INSERT ... INTO` and `SELECT ... INTO #temp` now keep reference edges (#655)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now treats statement-spanning SQL target matches as multiline patterns, so `INSERT` / `INTO` and `SELECT` / `INTO #temp` forms split across lines keep their `reference` edges instead of falling back to same-line-only behavior. Added a regression that covers split-line `INSERT INTO audit_log`, split-line `SELECT ... INTO #selected_users`, and the later `SELECT * FROM #selected_users` readback. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`. Fixes #655.
- **SQL temp-object gating now stays statement-order and ignores double-quoted dynamic SQL (#659, #706)** — `ReferenceExtractor` keeps temp-object establishment tied to the current SQL statement/body instead of letting later writes backfill earlier reads, and the double-quoted dynamic-SQL path continues to stay opaque for temp-table establishment. Added focused extractor and CLI regressions for a later-body `#temp` write and the double-quoted dynamic-SQL repro. Affected: `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`.
- **Objective-C support now indexes `.m` / `.mm` files and extracts interfaces, protocols, properties, methods, and imports (#310)** — `FileIndexer` now routes `.m` and `.mm` to `objc`, `SymbolExtractor` has an `objc` pattern set for `@interface`, `@implementation`, `@protocol`, `@property`, method signatures, and `#import` / `#include`, and both the CLI `languages` output and regression tests now surface the new language. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/FileIndexer.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/FileIndexerTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Fixes #310.
- **Kotlin `fun` declarations now recognize inheritance modifiers (#324)** — `SymbolExtractor` now accepts `abstract`, `override`, `open`, and `final` in Kotlin function modifier sequences, so inherited and overridable functions are indexed again instead of being skipped. Added a regression that covers abstract base members, overriding members, `final` overrides, and modifier combinations with visibility and `suspend`. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Fixes #324.
- **SQL `UPDATE TOP (...)` now keeps the real target instead of indexing `TOP` (#654)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now treats `UPDATE` as a dedicated SQL target form so optional `TOP (...)` modifiers do not get mistaken for the mutated object. Added a focused regression covering `UPDATE TOP (10) dbo.Users`, `UPDATE TOP (1) ##session_log`, and the plain `UPDATE dbo.Users` control. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #654.
- **`cdidx find` now accepts `--max-line-width 0` as the no-truncation mode (#980)** — `find` previously rejected zero with a usage error even though the CLI's compatibility contract treats `0` as "disable truncation". The validation path now routes `--max-line-width` through the non-negative integer parser, so `0` works again while other positive-only limits keep their existing checks.
- **CommonJS multiline brace bodies now keep their symbol ranges in `symbols` output (#904)** — `symbols` now preserves `start_line`, `end_line`, `body_start_line`, and `body_end_line` for direct CommonJS named exports whose function bodies open on the next line, so Allman-style `module.exports.foo = function ()` and `module.exports.bar = () =>` forms stay queryable instead of flattening to the declaration line. Added a CLI regression that indexes a temp JavaScript file and checks the JSON `symbols` payload. Affected: `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`.
- **Kotlin `enum class` entries now show up as symbols (#320)** — `SymbolExtractor` now captures indented Kotlin enum entries such as `NORTH,`, `OK(200),`, and bodied entries like `RED(0xFF0000) { ... }` as `property` symbols, so enum values and per-entry override members are visible again in `symbols` and related navigation. Added regression coverage for plain, parameterized, and bodied entries, plus the trailing `fun` / `abstract fun` members that follow the enum body. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`.
- **TypeScript ambient declarations and overloads now index correctly (#236)** — `SymbolExtractor` now captures `declare function` / `declare const` forms, classifies `type` aliases as `interface`, skips same-line decorators before method parsing, and collapses overload signatures so the implementation row is kept. Added focused regressions for ambient declarations, decorated methods, and overload collapsing. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`, `CHANGELOG.md`.
- **Plain C# interpolated strings no longer hide later helpers from symbol extraction (#882)** — `SymbolExtractor` now keeps plain `$"..."` strings that contain nested string literals inside interpolation holes from desynchronizing the C# lexer, so later helpers in the same file keep showing up in `symbols` and `definition`. Added a focused regression for the DbReader-style SQL fixture. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #882.
- **Kotlin secondary constructors now index as class-named function symbols (#316)** — `SymbolExtractor` now captures `constructor(...)` declarations inside Kotlin class bodies, then normalizes them to the enclosing class name after container assignment so `definition`, `callers`, and `outline` include alternate construction paths. Added regressions for overloaded constructors, brace-less constructor chaining, and private secondary constructors. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #316.
- **Java generic bounds now surface real bound references, and real short type names no longer get suppressed by generic-parameter spelling heuristics (#642, #644)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now indexes Java type-parameter bounds such as `class Demo<T extends Root & Bound>` and `<U extends Wrapper<Root>>`, keeping the bound types visible as `type_reference` edges. At the same time, the old spelling-based generic-parameter filter no longer hides real short names like `X` or `TResult`, so C# declaration positions keep surfacing genuine type names instead of dropping them as presumed generic parameters. Added focused regressions for Java generic bounds, Java nested generic bounds, and short C# type names. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`.
- **C# nested lambda declaration-pattern shadowing stays scoped to the lambda, preserving outer qualified enum-member edges (#562)** — Added a tighter regression for the `values.Any(value => value is Holder RealNs && values.Any(other => other is Holder Other))` shape so the declaration-pattern receiver stays inside the nested lambda and the outer `RealNs.Status.Ready` references remain visible in both extractor-level and CLI exact-name JSON paths. This pins the same shadowing family as the existing lambda and static-lambda regressions, but exercises a deeper lambda nesting shape that better matches the reported false negative. Affected: `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`.
- **`references --count` now stays count-like for the C# `using static` suppression path, and `--max-line-width` no longer changes which rows are suppressed (#688, #699)** — The exact C# `using static` fallback now keeps the count path on a leaner raw-row query instead of paying for ordered search-result pagination, so `references --count` no longer drags in the full search shape just to compute totals. The same suppression path continues to inspect the raw database context before any line-width clamping, so display-width options cannot resurrect a false positive. Added regressions that cover the count total on a suppressed-heavy fixture and the tiny-width CLI repro. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/DbReaderTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Closes #688, #699.
- **C# named tuple return types inside generic arguments now keep their element-name spacing (#652)** — `SymbolExtractor` no longer collapses the separator space between a tuple element type and its element name when it normalizes generic-return-type text, so shapes like `Dictionary<string, (int x, int y)>` and `Task<((int A, int B), string Name)>` keep readable `return_type` values instead of merging tokens into `intx` / `stringName`. Added extractor regressions covering method and property return types with named tuples, plus the existing unnamed tuple case to keep the canonical compact form intact. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`.
- **C# `using static` same-name type-pattern regressions are now covered (#685, #691)** — Added regressions that keep real types with the same short name as `using static`-imported enum/const names from being mistaken for those imports in pattern positions such as `value is Red` and `case Red =>`, so `references` / `inspect` / `impact` preserve genuine type dependencies in both top-level and nested cases. Affects: `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`.
- **C# when-guard calls after split positional patterns stay visible (#986)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now keeps the real `call` edge for guards such as `when Check()` after split positional pattern heads, and a focused regression test covers the multiline `when`-guard case. Affects: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`.

### [1.16.0] - 2026-04-30

#### Added
- **Migrated CodeIndex to FSL-1.1-ALv2 with explicit integration and competing-use policy docs** — Replaced the repository-level licensing notice with FSL-1.1-ALv2, added canonical `LICENSES/FSL-1.1-ALv2.txt` and `LICENSES/Apache-2.0.txt`, documented non-competing AI/IDE/MCP/CI integration permissions in `INTEGRATION_POLICY.md`, clarified competing-use terms in `COMMERCIAL_LICENSE.md`, updated `TRADEMARKS.md`, `README.md`, `src/CodeIndex/CodeIndex.csproj`, `install.sh`, and release/license-policy workflows, and packaged the new policy files with official releases and NuGet output.
- **Installer `--doctor [vX.Y.Z]` network diagnostics with credential-safe proxy env output (#436, #914)** — `install.sh --doctor` now prints the active proxy environment variables (`HTTP_PROXY` / `HTTPS_PROXY` / `ALL_PROXY` / `NO_PROXY` plus their lowercase siblings) and probes the upstream URLs the installer would hit (the latest-release API endpoint, the release tarball asset, and the `sha256sums.txt` checksums asset) using `curl -sSI`. Each value is routed through a new `redact_proxy_userinfo` helper so URL userinfo such as `http://alice:hunter2@proxy:8080` surfaces as `http://<redacted>@proxy:8080` — keeping the host/port visible for reachability diagnosis while ensuring `--doctor` output stays safe to paste into logs / issues / support transcripts (the doctor is primarily used to diagnose blocked installs and its output is likely to be shared externally). Credential-less proxy URLs and bare-host `NO_PROXY` values are not rewritten, so the redaction does not misleadingly advertise credentials that were never there. Each probe reports its HTTP status, and on `CONNECT tunnel failed, response 403` (`curl` exit 56) the doctor reuses the existing upstream-proxy guidance so users get a single, actionable next step without needing prior network knowledge: the deny is in an upstream proxy/egress policy before TLS, route substitution alone will not fix it, ask the network team to allow-list at least one artifact host path, or point `CDIDX_GITHUB_BASE_URL` / `CDIDX_GITHUB_API_BASE_URL` at a reachable internal mirror. The version argument is optional — when omitted, the doctor reads the bundled `version.json`; when no version is available either, it still runs the API probe and clearly reports the release-asset probes as skipped so a missing version never masquerades as a probe against a bogus `v0.0.0` URL. Does not install anything, never writes outside `/tmp`, and does not short-circuit on the first failure so one network-policy deny does not hide others. Exits 0 when every probe returns 2xx/3xx, exits 1 otherwise. Tests cover the all-200 success path, the CONNECT-tunnel 403 advisory path, explicit-version + `v`-prefix normalization, the `version.json` fallback, the no-version skip path, the HTTP-4xx-reachability-not-confirmed path, the proxy-env surfacing path, the URL-userinfo redaction path (covering `user:password@host`, `user@host`, and SOCKS5 shapes plus the negative case where no credentials are present), the invalid-version-pass-through diagnostic shape, and the dispatcher wiring via a PATH-stubbed `curl` / `uname` shim. Affected: `install.sh`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/InstallScriptTests.cs`, `README.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`, `CLOUD_BOOTSTRAP_PROMPT.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`. Closes #436, #914.
- **JavaScript/TypeScript export-surface extraction now handles same-line minified re-exports and CommonJS bindings (#903)** — `SymbolExtractor` now keeps barrel re-export module names tied to the current statement start instead of the first `from` on the line, so same-line minified forms like `export*from './a';export{foo as bar}from './b';export*as ns from './c';` preserve both the `import` row and the exported property surface. CommonJS named export bindings now also recover same-line and multiline parenthesized wrappers correctly, and TypeScript generic-arrow bindings keep their function classification when the right-hand side starts on a later line. Added focused regressions for same-line minified re-exports, same-line CommonJS assignments, multiline CommonJS assignments, parenthesized CommonJS wrappers, and TypeScript generic-arrow CommonJS exports. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #903.

#### Fixed
- **Java generic methods now index correctly, and switch expressions no longer emit phantom `function switch` / `function <TypeName>` rows (#225)** — `SymbolExtractor` now accepts Java type-parameter prefixes such as `public <T> T first(...)` / `public <K, V> V get(...)`, and no longer mistakes statement keywords such as `return` or `case` for method signatures. Added regression coverage for Java 21 switch expressions and pattern arms. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Fixes #225.
- **Java JPMS module directives now emit reference graph edges (#883)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now records `requires`, `uses`, and `provides ... with ...` in `module-info.java` as `type_reference` edges, so `references` and other graph queries can see module dependencies again. Added focused extractor and CLI regressions for `requires`, `uses`, and `provides` on a module descriptor. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Closes #883.
- **C# `when`-guard calls after split positional patterns now stay visible (#986)** — `ReferenceExtractor` no longer suppresses a real `when Check()` call just because the previous line ends with `case`, `is`, or `not`, so split positional patterns keep their phantom pattern-head suppression while guard calls still land as `call` rows. Added a focused regression for `case` + split `Point(var x, var y) when Check()` coverage. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #986.
- **Reference text now lives once per file/line in `reference_lines`, shrinking fresh indexes and keeping legacy DBs readable** — `DbWriter` now normalizes shared line context out of `symbol_references` and stores it in a dedicated `reference_lines` table keyed by `(file_id, line)`. `DbReader` and `DbSymbolReader` now read from the normalized table when it exists, while still falling back to legacy `symbol_references.context` on older databases so existing `.cdidx/codeindex.db` files remain queryable until they are rebuilt. Added regression coverage for deduped storage, cleanup of `reference_lines` on file deletion, and both current-schema and legacy-schema read paths. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbContext.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbWriter.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbSymbolReader.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/DatabaseTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/DbReaderTests.cs`, `README.md`.
- **C# switch-expression positional patterns no longer emit phantom `call` edges (#968)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now suppresses the generic call edge when a C# `CallRegex` hit is the head of a positional switch-expression arm, so same-line and split-line forms such as `Point(var x, var y) => 1` and `Point(var c, var d)\n    => 2` keep the real `type_reference` for `Point` without adding a bogus `call Point` row that would pollute `references`, `callers`, `callees`, and `impact`. Added a focused regression that pins the `Point` `type_reference` count and asserts the phantom `call` row is absent. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #968.
- **JS/TS styled-factory gates now reject post-template comparison and division operators (#997)** — `SymbolExtractor` now treats `<`, `>`, and `/` as rejection operators after a real tagged template, so bindings like `styled.div\`...\` < theme`, `styled.div\`...\` > theme`, and `styled.div\`...\` / theme` no longer survive as phantom component symbols. Added focused JavaScript and TypeScript regressions for the post-template operator forms. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #997.
- **Bare verbatim `inspect` queries now fail closed before reaching caller/callee analysis (#952)** — `inspect` now rejects bare verbatim tokens like `@` up front and `DbReader` also returns empty caller/callee analysis for that input, so direct library calls cannot descend into an invalid query state. Added regressions for the CLI `inspect @ --exact --json` path and direct `DbReader` calls. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbSymbolReader.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/DbReaderTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Closes #952.
- **Kotlin / Swift / Scala deep nested triple bodies no longer unwind early at 4+ levels (#1002)** — `StructuralLineMasker` now keeps the 3+ deep triple state on a small stack and only exits when the matching opener/close shape is seen, so a 4th nested triple inside Kotlin raw strings, Swift multi-line strings, or Scala interpolated triples cannot terminate the deep mask too early and leak later source as phantom calls. Added focused regression tests for all three languages.
- **Indexing now emits an initial progress bar instead of leaving only `Indexing...` on short or fast runs** — `cdidx index` now prints a `0.0%` progress line as soon as the file list is known, so small or fast full scans still show a real progress bar instead of only the spinner banner. The spinner is paused while that first bar is rendered, and the existing throttled redraws continue to keep progress visible during the run. Added regressions for the initial `0.0%` render and the redirected-output full-scan banner. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Cli/IndexCommandRunner.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Cli/ConsoleUi.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ConsoleUiTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/IndexCommandRunnerTests.cs`.
- **Kotlin / Swift / Scala multi-line triple-quoted strings no longer leak phantom call references (#385, #992, #996, #1000, #1001)** — `StructuralLineMasker` now covers three new languages whose `"""..."""` bodies previously slipped past the single-line `StringLiteralRegex` and polluted `references` / `callers` / `callees` / `impact` / `inspect` / MCP `analyze_symbol`: Kotlin `"""..."""` raw strings (preserving `${expr}` interpolation holes so real calls stay visible; bare `$identifier` is masked with the body because a lone identifier cannot be a call), Swift plain `"""..."""` and extended `#"""..."""#` / `##"""..."""##` multi-line strings (with matching `\(expr)` / `\#(expr)` / `\##(expr)` interpolation holes preserved, and same-line extended raw strings `#"..."#` correctly scanned to the matching `"<hashes>` close), and Scala plain `"""..."""` plus interpolator-prefixed `s"""..."""` / `f"""..."""` / `raw"""..."""` forms (only interpolator-prefixed forms recognize `${expr}` holes; plain `"""..."""` has no interpolation and stays fully masked). Each masker tracks nested block comments (Kotlin, Swift, Scala all allow them), skips single-line strings and char literals before entering a triple open, and resumes masking without state confusion across lines. Hole scanners additionally recognize `//` line comments and `/* ... */` block comments inside the hole so a legal `${ /* } */ after() }` / `\( /* ) */ after() )` comment body cannot close the hole prematurely and drop `after()` from the reference graph. Hole scanners also recognize a nested triple-quoted opener (Kotlin `"""...`, Swift `"""...` / `#"""...`, Scala `"""...`) and the nested literal's own interpolation holes, so a `${innerCall()}` inside a Kotlin nested triple, a `\(innerCall())` inside a Swift nested triple, or a `${innerCall()}` inside an interpolator-prefixed Scala nested triple still preserves the real `innerCall()` edge while body chars between holes stay masked — closing the #992 follow-up where the nested body leaked as phantom references and the #996 follow-up where the nested triple's own real calls were silently dropped. Plain Scala nested `"""..."""` (no prefix) stays fully masked because plain Scala triples have no interpolation. The hole scanners also defensively track triple-quoted literals opened 3+ levels deep (i.e. inside the nested triple's own `${...}` / `\(...)` hole): a depth counter masks the deep body through to the matching close so phantom calls cannot leak even at 3+ deep nesting (real calls 4+ levels deep are sacrificed for masking soundness; full stack tracking would be needed to preserve them). The Swift `\(...)` outer hole branch also recognizes `#"..."#` extended raw strings, and a shared `MaskSwiftSingleLineRawString` helper now masks the body chars while preserving any matching `\#(expr)` interpolation hole bodies (so real call edges inside the raw string still reach the reference graph) — closing the #1001 follow-up where the previous full-mask path silently dropped real `\#(...)` call edges. The Swift deep-nesting depth counter also tracks the deep open's hash count and requires the same hash count at the close (closes #1000), so a deep `<N>#"""..."""<N>#` literal cannot exit deep masking at a stray bare `"""` and let later phantom calls leak. Added focused `ReferenceExtractor` regressions for each language covering the phantom-call scope, the interpolation-hole preservation scope, the hole-comment scope, the nested-triple-in-hole phantom scope, the nested-triple inner-hole real-call-preservation scope, the 3-deep nesting masking scope, and the Swift outer-hole hash-delimited raw-string scope. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/StructuralLineMasker.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #385, #992, #996, #1000, #1001.
- **Grouped `callers` / `callees` rows now expose distinct `reference_kinds` instead of collapsing mixed `call` / `subscribe` / `instantiate` sets to one label (#501)** — `DbReader.GetCallers` and `GetCallees` now aggregate `GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT reference_kind)` alongside the existing preferred-kind column, and `CallerResult` / `CalleeResult` expose the sorted `reference_kinds` array plus a `has_mixed_reference_kinds` bool. The scalar `reference_kind` is retained on both caller and callee JSON (back-compat with existing consumers) and carries the preferred summary kind (`instantiate` > `subscribe` > `MIN(call)`); AI clients that need the full picture read `reference_kinds` + `has_mixed_reference_kinds`. Human-readable CLI output joins mixed kinds with `+` (for example `call+subscribe`) in place of the single preferred label, and the reference-kind column widens dynamically to fit the longest label in the batch so mixed rows no longer overrun the neighbouring column. MCP tool descriptions document the new contract. This lets AI clients distinguish pure event subscriptions, pure constructor hits, and real mixed call-and-subscribe containers without chasing a second `--exact` query. Added focused DB and CLI regressions that pin mixed `call` + `subscribe` containers and keep single-kind rows reporting `has_mixed_reference_kinds=false`. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Models/QueryResults.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Cli/QueryCommandRunner.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Mcp/McpToolDefinitions.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/DbReaderTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `README.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`. Closes #501.
- **Concurrent readers no longer observe torn multi-statement status / map / analyze snapshots (#180)** — `DbReader.GetStatus`, `DbReader.AnalyzeSymbol` (CLI `inspect` / MCP `analyze_symbol`), and `RepoMapBuilder.Build` (CLI `map` / MCP `repo_map`) now each wrap their multi-SELECT body in a single `BEGIN DEFERRED` transaction so every sub-query resolves against the same WAL snapshot. Without this, a writer committing between the `files` and `symbol_references` COUNT(*) statements inside `GetStatus` let concurrent readers observe impossible states such as `files=836, refs=0` against a steady-state 44k-ref index during an incremental update. The deferred transaction acquires only a SHARED lock on the first SELECT, so it does not block other writers; it is committed explicitly at the end to release that lock promptly, and sub-queries that open their own `SqliteDataReader` are scoped in inner blocks so their handles close before `Commit()`. Added three bug-catching `ConcurrencyTests` regressions: `GetStatus` (`refs == files * refsPerFile` invariant), `AnalyzeSymbol` (`references.Count == callers.Count` plus `WorkspaceLatestModified` matches the refs-count state to also exercise `GetWorkspaceFreshness`), and `GetRepoMap` (`latest_modified == workspace_latest_modified` plus the `Entrypoints` set matches the freshness state to also exercise `GetEntrypoints`); each fails without the matching DEFERRED wrap and passes with it. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbSymbolReader.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Database/RepoMapBuilder.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ConcurrencyTests.cs`, `CLAUDE.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`, `TESTING_GUIDE.md`. Closes #180.
- **Python f-string format specifiers now keep later calls visible to reference extraction (#984)** — The single-line Python f-string masker no longer treats `#` inside an interpolation as a comment boundary, so legal format specifiers such as `f"{value:#x}"` no longer cut off the rest of the line and hide later calls like `real_call()`. Added a regression that pins the format-specifier case. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #984.
- **C# positional `case Type(...):` and `is Type(...)` patterns no longer emit phantom `call` edges (#663, #969)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now suppresses the generic call edge when a C# `CallRegex` hit is the head of a positional switch or `is` pattern, so `case Point(var x, var y)` and `value is Point(var x, var y)` keep the real `type_reference` for `Point` without adding bogus `call Point` rows that would pollute `references`, `callers`, `callees`, and `impact`. Added focused regressions that pin the `Point` `type_reference` count and assert the phantom `call` row is absent. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #663, #969.
- **`--max-line-width 0` help and completion text now advertises the no-clamp compatibility mode (#947, #963)** — `cdidx --help`, shell completion, README, and the MCP schema now consistently say that `0` disables clamping so older automation that uses `0` for full-width output can discover the supported escape hatch without reading source. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Cli/ConsoleUi.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Mcp/McpToolDefinitions.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ConsoleUiTests.cs`, `README.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`. Closes #947, #963.
- **SQL exact-name queries now preserve leading `@` while C# verbatim normalization stays C#-only (#651)** — `DbReader` now limits C# verbatim identifier normalization to `--lang csharp` and language-neutral queries, so SQL exact-name lookups like `--lang sql --name @count --exact-name` keep their leading `@` and still match persisted SQL symbols. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Closes #651.
- **Exact-name bare verbatim symbols now return zero instead of failing closed** — `symbols --exact-name --count` and `definition --exact-name --count` now treat a bare verbatim token like `@` as an exact empty query and return a zero-result JSON/count payload instead of a usage error, while non-exact bare verbatim inputs still fail closed. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Cli/QueryCommandRunner.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`.
- **C# verbatim normalization now works for name lookups even without `--lang` while SQLite open/backfill validation retry handling remains in place (#651, #954, #962, #971, #972)** — `DbReader` now normalizes C# verbatim identifier spellings for name-based lookups even when `--lang` is omitted, while non-C# queries preserve leading `@` characters. `DbContext` also keeps transient SQLite open retry/backoff behavior for the main connection path and the backfill-fold validation path, with regressions covering CLI, MCP, and connection setup behavior. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbContext.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Cli/QueryCommandRunner.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Mcp/McpToolHandlers.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/McpServerTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/DatabaseTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/LegacySchemaMigrationTests.cs`. Closes #651, #954, #962, #971, #972.
- **C# XML-doc attachment now ignores ordinary block-comment shells and declaration-header continuations (#875, #876, #988)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now refuses to open `/**` when it is lexically inside a normal `/* ... */` block comment, and it treats multiline declaration headers as non-attachable until their generic / continuation depth closes. The same guard also recognizes indented C# raw-string closing delimiters before scanning later block comments, so a raw string cannot leave the file stuck in raw-string mode and reopen the `/**` leak path. Added regressions for ordinary block-comment containment plus triple-slash and delimited generic-field-header negatives, along with an indented raw-string / block-comment follow-up and a raw-string close-line regression. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #875, #876, #988.
- **Raw-string closing lines no longer mask following comments (#988)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now adjusts raw-string terminator detection so a closing line with a trailing semicolon or whitespace does not treat the next comment line as raw-string body. Added a regression that checks raw-string close lines directly. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #988.
- **JavaScript / TypeScript HOC-wrapped and call-result component bindings now emit `function` symbols (#240)** — `SymbolExtractor` previously matched only two JS/TS binding shapes: plain `function Name(...)` declarations and arrow-function bindings of the form `const Name = (...) => ...` (the arrow row's `stopAfterFirstPatternMatch` requires a literal `=>` right after the `=`). Any PascalCase binding whose right-hand side is a call expression, tagged template, or plain identifier — for example `const Wrapped = React.memo(Component)`, `const Box = React.forwardRef(...)`, `const Lazy = React.lazy(() => import('./X'))`, `const Connected = connect(mapState)(MyComponent)`, `const Styled = styled.div` `` `color: red` ``, or `const WithAuth = withAuthentication(Home)` — fell through the regex list entirely and produced zero symbol rows. `definition`, `symbols`, `callers`, `callees`, `inspect`, and `outline` therefore reported nothing for the most common React HOC patterns, even though the bindings were the public API that dependent modules imported and dependent JSX rendered. A new JavaScript row and a matching TypeScript row now capture HOC-wrapped / call-result / tagged-template component bindings. The right-hand side is intentionally restricted to a narrow allowlist of known HOC prefixes — `React.memo(` / `React.forwardRef(` / `React.lazy(` (the `React.` branch is pinned to these three HOC APIs specifically; bare `React.` is NOT accepted, so non-HOC React API calls like `const Theme = React.createContext(null);`, `const Stable = React.useCallback(() => 1, []);`, `const Derived = React.useMemo(...)`, and `const Ref = React.createRef();` are NOT promoted to phantom `function` symbols), `styled.` / `styled(` / `styled` ``\` ``, and the bare calls `connect(` / `memo(` / `forwardRef(` / `lazy(` / `observer(` / `with<PascalCase>(` — combined with a PascalCase gate on the binding name (`[A-Z]\w*`). Ordinary PascalCase or ALL_CAPS constants whose RHS is not a recognised HOC (`const Config = loadConfig();`, `const ENV = process.env.NODE_ENV;`, `const API = 'https://example.com';`) are explicitly NOT promoted to phantom `function` symbols. The allowlist also excludes `= class extends ...`, so a class-expression binding like `const Widget = class extends React.Component {}` produces a single `class Widget` symbol from the synthetic class-expression pass instead of colliding with a duplicate `function Widget` from this row. The TypeScript arrow-function row and the TypeScript HOC row both accept an optional `: <type>` annotation between the binding name and `=`, so explicitly typed bindings land on the correct row: typed HOC bindings such as `const Connected: React.ComponentType<Props> = connect(mapState)(MyComponent)` still capture via the HOC row, and typed arrow bindings whose annotation itself contains a function type (`const Callback: (x: number) => number = (x) => x + 1`) still win the arrow row with `BodyStyle.Brace` because the annotation parser consumes text lazily so the regex backtracks past the `=>` inside the annotation and locks onto the real arrow operator after the real `=`. Both HOC rows are placed **after** the pre-existing arrow-binding row so a capitalized arrow binding (`const Foo = () => { ... }`) is still captured by the arrow pattern first and its `stopAfterFirstPatternMatch` prevents the HOC row from emitting a duplicate `BodyStyle.None` symbol on the same line. `BodyStyle.None` on the HOC row is an intentional trade-off: the RHS body span of an HOC call is not reliably line-trackable from the declaration line alone, and declaration-only visibility into the symbol is still strictly better than dropping the binding entirely. Added `Extract_JavaScript_DetectsHocWrappedComponentBindings` covering `forwardRef` / `memo(named)` / `memo(arrow)` / `lazy` / `connect(...)(...)` / `styled.div` `` ` ` `` / `withAuthentication(...)`; `Extract_JavaScript_HocBindingPatternSkipsLowercaseBindings` pinning that `count` / `total` / `config` do not emit phantom `function` symbols; `Extract_JavaScript_HocBindingPatternSkipsPascalCaseNonHocConstants` pinning that `Config` / `ENV` / `API` / `Total` are also rejected by the HOC-prefix allowlist; `Extract_JavaScript_HocBindingPatternSkipsNonHocReactApiCalls` pinning that non-HOC React APIs such as `React.createContext(...)`, `React.useCallback(...)`, `React.useMemo(...)`, and `React.createRef(...)` produce zero phantom `function` symbols on both the JavaScript and TypeScript rows (the `React.` branch is strict to `memo` / `forwardRef` / `lazy`); `Extract_TypeScript_HocBindingPatternAcceptsGenericTypeArgumentsOnReactHoc` pinning that TypeScript HOC calls with generic type arguments — `React.forwardRef<HTMLDivElement, Props>(...)`, `React.memo<Props>(...)`, `React.lazy<typeof X>(...)`, bare `forwardRef<T>(...)` / `memo<T>(...)` / `lazy<T>(...)` / `connect<State, Dispatch>(...)` / `observer<Props>(...)` / `with<Pascal><T>(...)`, nested-generic forms like `React.memo<Map<string, Props>>(...)` and `React.memo<Record<string, Map<string, Props>>>(...)` (three levels deep), and function-type arguments like `React.memo<(props: Props) => JSX.Element>(...)` — are still captured, so the narrow `React.(memo|forwardRef|lazy)` gate does not drop the extremely common TypeScript `<TypeArgs>(...)` shape (the narrow allowlist on the TypeScript row accepts one optional `<...>` token before the `(`, balancing up to three levels of nested angle brackets and allowing parenthesised segments inside a generic argument via a shared `TypeScriptOptionalHocTypeArgsPattern` constant whose alternation branches start with disjoint character classes to rule out catastrophic backtracking; the JavaScript row intentionally does NOT carry this optional token because JavaScript has no generic syntax and `const Result = memo < Props > (Component);` is a chained comparison / call expression — pinned by `Extract_JavaScript_HocBindingPatternDoesNotMatchComparisonShape` across `memo` / `forwardRef` / `lazy` / `connect` / `observer` / `with<Pascal>` / `React.memo`); `Extract_JavaScript_HocBindingPatternDoesNotCollideWithClassExpressionBinding` pinning that `const Widget = class extends React.Component {}` yields one `class Widget` symbol and no `function Widget`; `Extract_JavaScript_HocBindingPatternDoesNotShadowCapitalizedArrow` pinning that `const Foo = () => { ... }` remains a single symbol captured by the arrow row with a brace body range; `Extract_TypeScript_DetectsHocWrappedComponentBindingsWithTypeAnnotation` pinning the TypeScript HOC type-annotation path (`Connected: React.ComponentType<Props>`, `Styled: StyledComponent<'div', Theme>`); and `Extract_TypeScript_TypedArrowBindingPreservesBraceBody` pinning that a function-typed arrow binding (`const Callback: (x: number) => number = (x) => { ... }`) keeps its brace body range on the arrow row and is not shadowed by the HOC row. A follow-up review pass caught two additional codex-review blockers that are rolled into this entry: (1) `TypeScriptOptionalHocTypeArgsPattern` now balances one level of nested parens inside each generic-argument paren segment (`\((?:[^()]|\([^()]*\))*\)` at every angle-bracket nesting level) so real React callback-prop shapes such as `React.memo<(props: { onClick: (x: number) => void }) => JSX.Element>(Box)` still capture. The inner paren branch (`\([^()]*\)`) is disjoint from `[^()]` at its first character (`(` vs not `(`), so the paren balancer remains ReDoS-safe. Pinned by `Extract_TypeScript_HocBindingPatternAcceptsCallbackPropInsideFunctionTypeGeneric`. (2) A new `ShouldSkipJavaScriptTypeScriptStyledFactoryCandidate` post-match gate rejects the styled factory-capture (`const F = styled.div;`) and plain-call (`const F = styled(Component);`) shapes that the HOC regex branch `styled[.(`` ` ``]` otherwise accepts, because neither form produces a rendered component binding on the declaration line. The gate inspects the raw (unmasked) source line because `StructuralLineMasker.MaskJsTsTemplateLiteralContents` blanks template-literal delimiters — including the opening backtick — to space, so the masked `patternMatchLine` cannot distinguish `styled.div` `` ` ``color: red`` ` `` from `styled.div;`. The real tagged-template forms (`styled.div` `` ` ``color: red`` ` ``, `styled(Component)` `` ` ``color: blue`` ` ``, and TypeScript's `styled.div<Props>` `` ` ``color: blue`` ` ``) all carry a backtick on the same raw line and still capture. Pinned by `Extract_JavaScript_HocBindingPatternRequiresTaggedTemplateForStyled` and `Extract_TypeScript_HocBindingPatternRequiresTaggedTemplateForStyled`. (3) The styled factory-capture / plain-call gate initially read the full raw line with `rawLine.Contains('`')`, so a statement with an unrelated tagged template on the same physical line — for example `const StyledFactory = styled.div; const note = ` `` ` ``not a component`` ` `` `;` — let the backtick from `const note = ...` re-open the gate for the earlier `styled.div;` binding and reintroduced the phantom `function StyledFactory` symbol the gate was meant to reject. The gate now scans only between the match's absolute end position (`lineOffset + match.Index + match.Length`) and the next `;` (or end-of-line), so any backtick that lives on a different statement of the same line is out of scope and cannot undo the rejection. The `lineOffset` argument threads the pattern's per-line offset through so the cursor starts at the match's real column in the original raw line. Pinned by `Extract_JavaScript_HocBindingPatternRejectsSameLineMultiStatementStyledFactory` and `Extract_TypeScript_HocBindingPatternRejectsSameLineMultiStatementStyledFactory`. (4) The statement-local scan was still pure raw character iteration, so it misinterpreted commented-out or stringified backticks and `;` characters in four symmetric shapes: a line comment whose body contains a backtick (`const F = styled.div // \`not a template\``), a block comment whose body contains a backtick (`const F = styled.div /* \`not a template\` */;`), a plain string literal whose body contains a backtick (`const F = styled.div + "\`";`), and — the mirror failure — a block comment whose body contains a `;` placed before a real tagged template on the same statement (`const F = styled.div /* ; */ \`color:red\`;`). The first three false-accepted phantom `function` symbols for non-template bindings; the fourth false-rejected a real tagged-template binding because the gate treated the comment-internal `;` as the statement terminator. The scanner now walks the raw line with basic JS/TS lexer awareness — `//` breaks out as end-of-statement, `/* ... */` is skipped (including the unclosed tail case, which also falls through as end-of-statement), and `"..."` / `'...'` literals are skipped with minimal escape handling (`\\` consumes the next character) — so only real source-code characters contribute to the accept/reject decision. Pinned by `Extract_JavaScript_HocBindingPatternIgnoresStyledBacktickInCommentsAndStrings` and `Extract_TypeScript_HocBindingPatternIgnoresStyledBacktickInCommentsAndStrings`. (5) The statement-local scanner was still single-line only, so Prettier / dprint formatted styled bindings whose tagged-template backtick sits on the line after `styled.div` / `styled(Component)` — e.g. `const Card = styled.div\n  \`color: red;\`;` and `const Link = styled(Anchor)\n  \`color: blue;\`;` — never found a real-code backtick on the match line and were rejected as factory-capture phantoms. Conversely an initial keyword-allowlist approach to terminating the multi-line scan (`const` / `let` / `var` / ... on the next line triggers ASI) could not detect identifier-start expression statements such as `const X = styled.div\nfoo(\`x\`)` or `const X = styled.div\nawait foo(\`x\`)` — the next line starts with an identifier, not a keyword, so the gate walked into the unrelated expression's backtick and re-accepted the factory-capture binding as a phantom `function StyledFactory`. The gate now walks across physical lines within a bounded lookahead window, carrying block-comment state across line boundaries so a `/* ... */` that straddles lines still masks correctly, and on each continuation line inspects the first non-whitespace non-comment character: only backtick (the tagged template itself) or `.` (member chain such as `.attrs\`...\``) may continue the expression.
