Any other starting character — identifier-start expression statements (`foo(\`x\`)`), top-level `await`, declaration keywords (`const Y = 5;`), JSX-element / TS-cast starts (`<Foo>{...}`), or any other non-continuation token — is treated as ASI-inserted statement termination and causes the gate to reject, so `const X = styled.div\nfoo(\`x\`)`, `const X = styled.div\nawait foo(\`x\`)`, `const X = styled.div\nconst Y = 5;`, and `const X = styled.div\n<Foo>{\`not a template\`}</Foo>` all stay rejected while `const X = styled.div\n  \`color: red;\`;` is accepted. `<` is intentionally NOT whitelisted as a continuation starter because styled-components generics always appear before the backtick on the same expression, never on a new line — so a continuation `<...>` is always either a JSX element or a TS cast, i.e. a new statement inserted by ASI. Pinned by `Extract_JavaScript_HocBindingPatternAcceptsMultiLineTaggedTemplateContinuation`, `Extract_TypeScript_HocBindingPatternAcceptsMultiLineTaggedTemplateContinuation` (the TS variant additionally pins `MultiLineAnnotated` so a `: ComponentType<Props>` type annotation on the binding name does not confuse the gate), `Extract_JavaScript_HocBindingPatternRejectsExpressionStatementWithBacktickOnNextStatement`, `Extract_TypeScript_HocBindingPatternRejectsExpressionStatementWithBacktickOnNextStatement`, `Extract_JavaScript_HocBindingPatternRejectsJsxElementOnNextStatement`, and `Extract_TypeScript_HocBindingPatternRejectsJsxElementOnNextStatement`. (6) Once the gate walked across physical lines, the inner accept-on-first-backtick step still did not check whether the backtick was actually in tag-head position on the current expression. A binding of the form `const NotStyledMember = styled.div + ` `` ` ``not a styled template`` ` ``;` (or `const NotStyledCall = styled(Component) + ` `` ` ``also not`` ` ``;`, including the typed-annotation variant `const AnnotatedNotStyled: StyledComponent<'div'> = styled.div + ` `` ` ``still not`` ` ``;`) walked through the `+` operator, found the backtick on the same statement, and accepted the candidate as a phantom `function NotStyledMember` / `function NotStyledCall` / `function AnnotatedNotStyled`. The scan now tracks paren / bracket / angle / brace depth so a backtick inside a nested expression (e.g. `.attrs({ ... })`) does not count as the tag head, and rejects the candidate the moment it sees a depth-0 operator character (`+`, `-`, `*`, `%`, `?`, `!`, `&`, `|`, `^`, `=`, `,`, `:`) between the match end and the first backtick. When the regex match already consumed an opening paren (`styled(`, `memo(`, `connect(`, etc.) the depth counter starts at -1 so the upcoming matching `)` restores it to 0 rather than going further negative — Prettier-style multi-line `styled(Component)\n` `` ` ``...`` ` `` shapes are still accepted. The `<` token is also treated as a depth-bumping bracket inside the gate, so even though TypeScript generics like `styled.div<Props>` `` ` ``...`` ` `` are written before the backtick on the same expression they balance to depth 0 by the time the scan reaches the backtick. Pinned by `Extract_JavaScript_HocBindingPatternRejectsOperatorBetweenStyledAndTemplate` and `Extract_TypeScript_HocBindingPatternRejectsOperatorBetweenStyledAndTemplate`. (7) The depth-tracking gate from (6) accepted on the **first** depth-0 backtick and immediately returned, so a binding of the form `const NotStyledPlusTheme = styled.div` `` ` ``color: red`` ` `` ` + theme;` (or the call-form `const NotStyledCallPlusTheme = styled(Component)` `` ` ``color: blue`` ` `` ` + theme;`, including the typed-annotation variant `const AnnotatedNotStyledPlusTheme: StyledComponent<'div'> = styled.div` `` ` ``color: red`` ` `` ` + theme;`) was promoted to a phantom `function NotStyledPlusTheme` symbol — the gate saw the tag-head backtick, accepted, and never noticed the post-template `+` operator that turns the right-hand side into a binary composition expression. Separately, the gate's lookahead window was capped at 8 physical lines, which is too small for Prettier-formatted bindings whose `.attrs((props) => ({ ... }))` argument object spans more than 10 lines before the backtick — those bindings ran past the window and were silently rejected. The gate now (a) walks across the **entire** template body — including `${ ... }` substitutions and across raw line boundaries with `\\` escape handling — when it sees the depth-0 tag-head backtick, sets a `tagHeadConsumed` flag, and continues scanning so that a depth-0 operator character (`+`, `-`, `*`, `%`, `?`, `!`, `&`, `|`, `^`, `=`, `,`, `:`) appearing **after** the closing backtick still rejects, while a depth-0 `;` after the closing backtick accepts the styled binding cleanly; (b) bumps the lookahead window from 8 to 32 physical lines so realistic Prettier-formatted `.attrs((props) => ({ ... }))` chains still resolve to the trailing tagged template. Two correctness fixes ride along: continuation-line first-character checking is now skipped while `depth > 0`, because lines 2+ of a multi-line nested-expression body (e.g. `field1: props.value1,` inside `.attrs((props) => ({ ... }))`) are expression continuation rather than a new statement and ASI does not insert there; and the arrow function token `=>` is now recognized as a single unit so the `>` does not falsely decrement depth and expose subsequent depth-0 operator characters inside arrow bodies (e.g. `(props) => prop1 ? a : b`) to spurious rejection. Pinned by `Extract_JavaScript_HocBindingPatternRejectsOperatorAfterStyledTemplate`, `Extract_TypeScript_HocBindingPatternRejectsOperatorAfterStyledTemplate`, and `Extract_TypeScript_HocBindingPatternAcceptsLongAttrsChainBeforeTemplate`. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #240.

### [1.15.3] - 2026-04-25

#### Fixed
- Fix the missed version update in version.json.

### [1.15.2] - 2026-04-25

#### Fixed
- **MCP notifications now stay silent instead of returning `id: null` errors (#186)** — `McpServer` now always short-circuits JSON-RPC notifications before emitting an error envelope, so unknown `notifications/*` messages no longer return `-32601 Method not found` with `id: null`. Known handshake notifications remain silent, and unknown notifications are logged to stderr for observability. Added regression coverage for silent stdout and stderr logging. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Mcp/McpServer.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/McpServerTests.cs`. Fixes #186.
- **JavaScript / TypeScript line-continuation strings outside template holes no longer leak phantom references (#844, #970)** — `StructuralLineMasker` now keeps top-level JS/TS single- and double-quoted strings alive across physical line boundaries the same way it already handled template-hole continuations, so `identifier(` text inside a backslash-continued string cannot survive into `references`, `callers`, or `impact`. Added regression coverage for JavaScript with CRLF continuation and TypeScript with a normal continued string, plus a for-of scan-buffer guard for continued single-quoted text. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/StructuralLineMasker.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #844. Closes #970.
- **SQL FROM lists now keep later tables after derived-table sources (#942, #974)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now treats parenthesized derived tables as skippable source-list items, so later comma-separated tables such as `accounts` in `SELECT * FROM (SELECT 1) x, accounts;` remain visible to `references` and graph queries instead of stopping at the derived-table item. The existing table-hint handling from #941 stays covered by the current regressions, and column-alias-list cases are covered by the same source-list path. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #942, #974.
- **PowerShell scope-prefixed functions and DSC/workflow declarations now extract correctly (#254, #308)** — `SymbolExtractor` now strips `script:` / `global:` / `local:` / `private:` from `function` / `filter` declaration names, and it also recognizes `configuration`, `workflow`, `using namespace`, and `using assembly` as PowerShell symbols. Added a focused regression that covers the plain and scope-prefixed function forms plus the additional top-level declaration forms. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`, `README.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #254, #308.
- **C# exact `references` now keep preserved `is`-pattern call rows while still suppressing `case` constant-pattern phantoms (#890)** — The exact C# static-constant pattern suppress path now distinguishes `case` and `is` anchors before removing call rows, so `references Red --exact-name --lang csharp` keeps the real `value is Color.Red or Color.Blue` hit while continuing to drop the same-file phantom from qualified `case Color.Red ...` patterns. Added a CLI regression that pins the `is`-pattern call row in `QueryCommandRunnerTests`. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Closes #890.
- **SymbolExtractor exact-duplicate suppression now uses hash-based per-file bookkeeping (#517)** — `AddSymbolRecord` now tracks exact symbol identities and same-line signature occurrences in per-file hash maps instead of rescanning the accumulated list on every append, preserving the same-line restart duplicate guard while avoiding the quadratic hot path. Added a regression that keeps the surviving same-line `Child` row singular in the `#571`-style fixture and a skipped large-fixture smoke test for symbol-volume performance. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/PerformanceTests.cs`. Closes #517.
- **`--max-line-width 0` help and completion text now advertises the no-clamp compatibility mode (#963)** — `cdidx --help`, shell completion, README, and the MCP schema now consistently say that `0` disables clamping so older automation that uses `0` for full-width output can discover the supported escape hatch without reading source. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Cli/ConsoleUi.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Mcp/McpToolDefinitions.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ConsoleUiTests.cs`, `README.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`.
- **`status` summary freshness now tracks indexed and source timestamps instead of wall-clock age (#928)** — `status` no longer labels a clean index stale just because it was built more than a few minutes ago. The one-line summary now compares the stored `indexed_at` and `latest_modified` timestamps, so a workspace is fresh when the index is at least as new as the source snapshot and stale only when the source is newer or the workspace is dirty. Added regression coverage for both the fresh and stale timestamp cases. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Cli/QueryCommandRunner.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `README.md`. Closes #928.
- **CommonJS named-export comparisons no longer look like assignments (#856)** — The shared JS/TS named-export regex now requires a real assignment operator, so top-level comparisons such as `module.exports.foo === undefined;`, `exports.bar == null;`, and `module.exports.baz !== 1;` no longer emit fake exported properties. Added JS/TS regressions covering the comparison cases. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #856.
- **`outline` now computes nested symbol depth from the container chain (#194)** — `outline` output no longer flattens every nested symbol to one level. `DbReader.GetOutline` now derives a per-symbol `Depth` from the stored container chain, human-readable outline output indents by that depth, and the JSON / MCP payloads expose the same field for AI clients. Added regressions for the nested depth chain in both `DbReaderTests` and `QueryCommandRunnerTests`. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbSymbolReader.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Models/QueryResults.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Cli/QueryCommandRunner.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/DbReaderTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `README.md`. Closes #194.
- **Single-line interpolated string references are preserved again** — `ReferenceExtractor` now restores call expressions inside single-line interpolated strings / template literals for C#, JavaScript, TypeScript, and Python before call scanning, so `references` / `callers` no longer drop hits like `Run()` in `$"..."`, `` `...${run()}` ``, or `f"..."`. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`.
- **Python f-strings now keep interpolation calls visible to reference extraction (#264)** — `PrepareLine()` now masks single-line Python f-string text while preserving `{...}` expression contents, so calls such as `run()` inside `f"value = {run()}"` stay visible to the reference extractor instead of being erased with the surrounding literal text. Added a regression that pins the call edge inside the interpolation. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #264.
- **Raw FTS query syntax errors now report usage errors instead of database errors (#187)** — `search --fts` now classifies FTS5 parser failures such as `tokio::spawn`, `AND OR`, and unterminated quoted input as query-authoring mistakes instead of database failures. The CLI prints a dedicated `FTS5 query syntax` message, returns `CommandExitCodes.UsageError`, and adds a hint that explains when `:` is being treated as an FTS5 column qualifier. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Cli/QueryCommandRunner.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `README.md`. Closes #187.
- **SQLite connection setup now retries transient busy/locked errors during concurrent reads** — `DbContext` now retries a few times when opening a connection or registering SQLite functions fails with a transient `SQLITE_BUSY` / `SQLITE_LOCKED` condition, which makes the concurrent-read test path more stable on Linux CI without changing the observable database contract. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbContext.cs`.
- **Long single-line query payloads can now be left untruncated with `--max-line-width 0` (#830)** — `search`, `references`, `find`, `excerpt`, and `inspect` now accept `--max-line-width 0` as an explicit full-line mode, so long Markdown or audit lines can be reviewed without `...(+N)...` elision when the caller needs the entire physical line. The MCP counterparts now expose the same zero-as-full-line contract through `maxLineWidth: 0`, `LineWidthFormatter` preserves the full line in that mode, and the CLI help/docs now describe the no-truncation escape hatch. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/LineWidthFormatter.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Cli/QueryCommandRunner.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Cli/ConsoleUi.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Mcp/McpToolHandlers.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Mcp/McpToolDefinitions.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SearchSnippetFormatterTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/McpServerTests.cs`, `README.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`. Closes #830.
- **C# exact `references` now preserve type-alias hits even when `using static` makes the same name look like a constant pattern (#715)** — `DbReader` now resolves active C# `using Alias = ...` scopes, including `global using`, through the current namespace before deciding whether they point at a real type, so `references Red --exact-name --lang csharp` keeps `using Red = RealTypes.Red;` hits without mistaking namespace aliases for type aliases. Added regression coverage for the CLI exact-name/count path. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Closes #715.
- **C# `using static` same-name type references now survive alias and nested scope checks (#693)** — `references` / `inspect` / `impact` now keep alias-scoped real types visible even when a `using static` constant shares the same short name, and C# same-file constant-pattern suppression no longer hides nested real types that shadow imported enum members. Added direct regressions for the alias-scoped `Probe.Shapes.Red` case and the nested `Outer.Red` case. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/DbReaderTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #693.
- **Bare verbatim `inspect` queries now fail closed before caller/callee analysis (#952)** — `inspect` now rejects a bare verbatim token `@` up front, and `DbReader` returns empty caller/callee analysis for that input, preventing direct library calls from descending into an invalid query state. Added regressions for the CLI `inspect @ --exact --json` path and direct `DbReader` calls. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbSymbolReader.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/DbReaderTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Closes #952.
- **MCP JSON-RPC responses preserve explicit `id: null` and reject boolean ids (#204)** — The MCP server now distinguishes missing `id` members from explicit `null`, so requests such as `{"id": null, ...}` return success/error responses that preserve `id: null`. Invalid requests with boolean `id` values return `-32600` and `id: null` instead of echoing the boolean. Added regressions for both null-id and boolean-id cases. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Mcp/McpServer.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/McpServerTests.cs`. Fixes #204.
- **C# positional `case Type(...):` and `is Type(...)` patterns no longer emit phantom `call` edges (#663)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now suppresses generic call edges when a C# `CallRegex` hit is the head of a switch positional pattern or `is` pattern, so `case Point(var x, var y)` and `value is Point(var x, var y)` keep the real `Point` `type_reference` without adding `call Point` rows that pollute `references` / `callers` / `callees` / `impact`. Added a focused regression that pins the `Point` `type_reference` count and the absence of phantom `call` rows. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #663.

### [1.15.1] - 2026-04-24

#### Fixed
- **Release artifacts now ship license notices and the installer preserves them** — `create-release` now copies `LICENSE`, `COMMERCIAL_LICENSE.md`, and `TRADEMARKS.md` into the published payload, and `install.sh` installs those notices when they are present alongside the binary.

### [1.15.0] - 2026-04-24

#### Changed
- **Hardened licensing and distribution policy against unauthorized productized forks** — Replaced the package and repository licensing metadata with PolyForm Perimeter 1.0.0, added commercial-use and trademark policy documents, embedded the policy files into NuGet packages and release payloads, gated official release publishing to the canonical repository, added `cdidx license`, and documented legacy package handling. Affected: `LICENSE`, `COMMERCIAL_LICENSE.md`, `TRADEMARKS.md`, `README.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`, `src/CodeIndex/CodeIndex.csproj`, `src/CodeIndex/Cli/ConsoleUi.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Cli/ProgramRunner.cs`, `.github/workflows/release.yml`, `.github/workflows/license-policy.yml`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/LicensePolicyTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ConsoleUiTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ProgramCliTests.cs`.

#### Fixed
- **SQL qualified non-exact graph readers no longer widen to longer qualifier chains (#766)** — `references` / `callers` / `impact` now keep qualified SQL lookups schema-scoped when they resolve a recorded call column, so `sales.proc_name` no longer matches `archive.sales.proc_name` through substring containment. The column-aware non-exact path now requires the same normalized segment count and an exact qualified-name match, matching the existing documentation contract and keeping longer qualifier chains out of qualified results. Added a regression that proves `sales.proc_name` stays empty while `archive.sales.proc_name` still resolves. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SqlNameResolver.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/DbReaderTests.cs`. Closes #766.
- **Graph-query JSON zero results now emit envelopes for non-exact misses (#798)** — `references`, `callers`, and `callees` now always print the structured zero-result JSON payload when `--json` is set, even when the graph table exists and the query misses without `--exact`. This keeps the machine-readable contract consistent with the exact and degraded zero-result paths and preserves freshness metadata for AI clients. Added CLI regressions for the non-exact zero-result path on all three commands. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Cli/QueryCommandRunner.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Closes #798.
- **Java annotation members with array defaults no longer look brace-bodied (#793)** — `FindJavaBraceRange` now treats `default { ... }` after a Java parameter list as an annotation default value instead of a real member body, so `value()` in `@interface Tags { String[] value() default {"a", "b"}; int age(); } class C {}` keeps `body_start_line` / `body_end_line` null and its signature closes at the semicolon while later same-line members still survive. Added a focused regression for the annotation member, sibling member, and trailing same-line class. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #793.
- **SQL `FROM` lists now keep later source tables instead of dropping comma-separated tails (#802)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now captures comma-separated `FROM` sources as a single SQL source-list match, so later tables such as `accounts` in `SELECT * FROM users, accounts;`, `accounts` in `SELECT * FROM users WITH (NOLOCK), accounts;`, and `events` in `SELECT * FROM public.logs l, [archive].[events] e;` remain visible to `references`, `inspect`, and graph-driven navigation instead of disappearing after the first comma. Added focused extractor regressions and kept the existing `JOIN` / `APPLY` / `DELETE ... USING` source handling intact. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #802.
- **C# enum member collisions no longer hide unused symbols (#701, #931, #932)** — `unused` now keeps C# enum members with duplicate names conservative instead of treating them as authoritative unused hits, so a reference to `Color.None` no longer suppresses `Status.None` in the same workspace. The collision guard now uses qualified container identity when available and applies the caller's path / test scope to peer checks, so out-of-scope duplicates do not distort scoped `unused` results. Unique enum members still appear normally. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbSymbolReader.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/DbReaderTests.cs`. Closes #701, #931, #932.
- **Regression coverage for C# switch-expression arm type references after guarded or relational earlier arms (#851, #852)** — Added extractor and built-CLI regressions that lock later switch-expression arm type heads as visible after an earlier `when`-guarded arm, after earlier recursive patterns with relational `<` / `>` operators, and after later generic arm heads such as `Wrapper<Point, Shape>`. Affected: `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Closes #851, #852.
- **SQL `DELETE ... USING` now keeps temp sources after a comma on the temp-table path, including mixed source lists and `using`-substring table names (#792)** — `ReferenceExtractor` no longer treats the comma tail after `DELETE FROM ... USING #staging_a AS a, #staging_b AS b`, the implicit-alias form `#staging_a a, #staging_b b`, or mixed lists such as `DELETE FROM audit_log USING reusing_data, #staging_b AS b` as a MySQL `# comment`, so later `#temp` sources continue to surface as `reference` edges instead of disappearing from the graph. Added regression coverage for both alias spellings plus the mixed regular-table / temp-table list, including a regular table whose name contains `using` as a substring. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #792.
- **Option-looking literals can now be passed as query text (#923)** — Query commands with positional query terms now accept `--query <query>` / `--query=<query>` and a one-token `-- <query>` escape, so users can search for literals such as `--path` without the parser treating them as CLI options. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Cli/QueryCommandRunner.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Cli/ConsoleUi.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `README.md`, `CLAUDE.md`.
- **Positional query literals that begin with `--` are now accepted in search (#799)** — `search` now treats the first unknown dash-prefixed positional token as the query text instead of rejecting it as an unsupported option, so quoted literals such as `--open-reports` can be searched directly alongside the existing `--query` and `-- <query>` escapes. Added parser and CLI regressions for the direct positional case. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Cli/QueryCommandRunner.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `README.md`. Closes #799.
- **Regression coverage now pins SQL double-quoted dotted `EXEC` / `EXECUTE` procedure targets (#722)** — Added extractor and DB-reader regressions for single double-quoted SQL identifiers that contain dots, such as `EXEC "sales.fn_Target";` and `EXECUTE "sales.fn_Target";`, so exact `references`, `callers`, and `impact` continue to distinguish that one quoted identifier from a real schema-qualified `sales.fn_Target`. Affected: `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/DbReaderTests.cs`. Closes #722.
- **Java same-line record and brace-valued annotation extraction no longer drops inline members or emits phantom record-header functions (#760, #761, #762, #801)** — `SymbolExtractor` now keeps Java same-line type-body scans anchored inside the actual class/record/enum body instead of reopening the header for later regex rows, and the Java method pattern no longer treats `record R(...)` headers as `function R` rows with `return_type = "record"`. Java compact-constructor extraction now advances across same-line record-body siblings, so `int first() { ... } public R { ... }`, `static int y; public R { ... }`, and `public R { ... } int later() { ... }` keep both the compact constructor and neighboring members visible. Added focused regressions for brace-literal annotation arguments on same-line methods, records, and enum-member bodies, one-line records with inline methods and compact constructors, compact constructors after same-line sibling statements, and same-line members after compact constructors. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #760, #761, #762, #801.
- **SQL `EXEC` / `EXECUTE` / `CALL` no longer misindex MySQL `# comment` tails as T-SQL temp stored procedures (#656)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now gates bare `#tempProc` / `##tempProc` after `EXEC` / `EXECUTE` / `CALL` on same-file T-SQL establishment (prior `CREATE TABLE #x`, `SELECT ... INTO #x`, `#x` mutation target, or the newly recognized `CREATE PROCEDURE|PROC|FUNCTION #x` with optional `OR REPLACE|ALTER` / `TEMP|TEMPORARY`). Without that evidence the proc-call extractor now drops the would-be call edge so MySQL / MariaDB line comments such as `CALL #commented_out;` / `EXEC #tempProc;` / `EXECUTE #tempExecute;` stop polluting `references` / `callers` / `callees` / `impact` / MCP graph tools, while quoted forms such as `EXEC [#tempProc];` and `` EXEC `#backtickProc`; `` bypass the gate because bracket / backtick quoting is unambiguously an identifier. `ShouldTreatHashAsSqlCommentCore` also allows `PROCEDURE` / `PROC` / `FUNCTION` / `CALL` to keep `#name` as an identifier so the establishment tracker can register temp routines before their later proc-call sites run through the gate, and `CanSqlStatementEstablishTempObject` recognizes `SqlCreateTempRoutineRegex` so semicolon-less `CREATE PROCEDURE|FUNCTION #x` lines still flush the temp name before the next-line `EXEC` / `CALL` runs through the gate. Added focused extractor regressions for the MySQL comment false-positive, the established-temp-routine positive path (including the semicolon-less `CREATE PROCEDURE|FUNCTION` shape), and the quoted-form bypass. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #656.
- **JavaScript/TypeScript symbol extraction now surfaces barrel re-exports and CommonJS export bindings across commented, multiline, minified, namespace, shorthand, spread-safe, non-identifier-key-safe, parenthesized, type-only-star, import-attributes, and TS-generic-arrow forms (#226, #848, #849, #858, #861, #864, #870, #871, #872, #885, #888, #895, #896, #905, #906, #908, #909, #916, #917, #921, #922)** — `SymbolExtractor` now emits symbol rows for JS/TS re-export surfaces such as `export * from`, `export * as ns from`, `export { foo as bar } from`, `export type { User } from`, `export type * from`, and `export type * as ns from`, including multiline star / namespace-star forms, standalone `export` + `{ ... } from` layouts, trailing-comment cases, same-line and multiline import-attributes suffixes such as `with { type: 'json' }` / `assert { type: 'json' }`, and minified valid syntax such as `export*from` / `export{foo as bar}from`. Those re-exports now preserve the source-module `import` rows as well as the exported property surface without letting comment text hijack the recovered module specifier after lex sanitization. It also surfaces direct CommonJS named exports like `module.exports.foo = function () {}`, `module.exports.bar = () => {}`, `module.exports.fn = <T>(x: T) => x`, `module.exports.foo = <T>(\n  value: T\n) => value`, `module.exports.foo = <T extends (...args: any[]) => number>(value: T) => value`, and `exports.baz = value` even when the right-hand side starts on a following line, is wrapped in same-line or multiline leading parentheses, appears inside top-level conditional blocks, uses async / constrained TypeScript generic-arrow headers, or puts the function body's opening `{` on a later line, while keeping identifier prefixes like `functionCall()` / `classyThing` classified as ordinary property values rather than false function/class exports. Exported object-literal alias and shorthand properties such as `module.exports = { foo: inner }` and `module.exports = { foo, bar }` stay visible without misclassifying top-level spread entries or quoted/computed key values as exported properties. This keeps `symbols`, `definition`, and related symbol-oriented queries from silently missing or corrupting public JS/TS API surfaces that were already exported but previously invisible to the index. Added focused extractor regressions for commented, multiline, minified, type-only-star, and import-attributes barrel re-exports, namespace re-exports, newline-separated `export` + `{ ... } from` forms, multiline named import-attribute layouts, re-export source-module import rows, comment-safe module recovery, same-line, multiline, parenthesized, constrained, async, and TS generic-arrow CommonJS named export assignments, brace-body range preservation, top-level conditional CommonJS exports, prefix-safe CommonJS RHS classification, exported object-literal alias/shorthand properties, quoted/computed object keys, and spread entries. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #226, #848, #849, #858, #861, #864, #870, #871, #872, #885, #888, #895, #896, #905, #906, #908, #909, #916, #917, #921, #922.

### [1.14.0] - 2026-04-24

#### Added
- **Regression coverage for C# metadata-target `signature` column fallback on legacy / read-only DBs (#431)** — `LegacySchemaMigrationTests` now pins the deps / impact path that exercises `BuildMetadataTargetKindExpr`'s `name LIKE '%Attribute'` fallback when `_symbolColumns` does not contain `signature`. The new fixture seeds a normal C# attribute class + `[Audit]` reference, drops the `signature` column, reopens the DB read-only (so `TryMigrateForRead` cannot re-add it), and asserts that `GetFileDependencies` still resolves the `User.cs → Audit.cs` edge via the attribute-suffix alias and `AnalyzeImpact` still surfaces `User.cs` as a heuristic consumer — closing the #431 test-coverage gap so any future regression that removes the column-guarded fallback surfaces as `SqliteException: no such column: s.signature` in CI rather than at query time. Affected: `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/LegacySchemaMigrationTests.cs`. Closes #431.

#### Fixed
- **`type_reference` kind contract now consistent across `references` / `callers` / `callees` (#444)** — `QueryCommandRunner.AllValidReferenceKinds` now includes the compile-time `type_reference` edge kind emitted by `ReferenceExtractor` for C#/Java base lists, declaration types, generic constraints, `throws`, `is`/`as`/`instanceof`, and XML-doc `cref`, so `cdidx references <name> --kind type_reference` no longer prints the contradictory `unknown reference kind` warning while still returning hits. A new `CallGraphOnlyReferenceKinds` set is used by `callers` / `callees` so those commands only accept `call` / `instantiate` / `subscribe`, and `TryRejectMetadataKindForGraphCommand` has been generalized to `TryRejectNonCallGraphKindForGraphCommand` (plus the MCP `IsNonCallGraphReferenceKind` / `BuildNonCallGraphKindRejectionMessage` helpers) so CLI and MCP both reject `--kind type_reference` (alongside `attribute` / `annotation`) on `callers` / `callees` with `CommandExitCodes.UsageError` and a hint redirecting users to `references --kind type_reference`. MCP `references` / `callers` / `callees` tool descriptions and `kind` schemas are updated to match the new contract. Added focused CLI and MCP regressions covering `references --kind type_reference` success, `callers` / `callees --kind type_reference` rejection, and the existing metadata-kind rejection paths. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Cli/QueryCommandRunner.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Mcp/McpToolHandlers.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Mcp/McpToolDefinitions.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/McpServerTests.cs`, `CLAUDE.md`. Closes #444.
- **Fold-only readiness degradation now surfaces concrete remediation in `index`/`status` instead of a generic warning (#290, #893)** — when `fold_ready=false` is the only remaining degraded readiness bit, human-readable `index` output now prints a fold-specific warning with an in-place `cdidx backfill-fold --db ...` hint and a full-rebuild fallback instead of only pointing users at `status --json`. `status --json` exposes `fold_ready_reason` plus fold-only `degraded_reason`, `recommended_action`, and `alternative_action`, and `index --json` now mirrors those same fold-specific remediation fields so post-index AI flows do not need a second `status` round-trip just to decide how to repair the DB. The one-line status summary now marks fold-only degradation as `DEGRADED`. Added focused CLI regressions for the fold-only `index` warning, incremental/update `index --json`, and both JSON/human `status` paths. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Cli/IndexCommandRunner.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Cli/QueryCommandRunner.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Models/QueryResults.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/IndexCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `README.md`. Closes #290, #893.
- **Fold-only remediation commands now normalize read-only `file:` DB URIs back to writable paths instead of echoing failing commands (#854, #867, #892)** — `status --json` no longer reuses explicit read-only `file:` DB URIs verbatim in `recommended_action` / `alternative_action`; absolute `file:///...?...immutable=1` / `mode=ro` inputs and relative `file:codeindex.db?...` inputs now both point at the writable filesystem path for the same DB, `cdidx index <project> --db file:...immutable=1` is rejected up front with an actionable error instead of reaching an unhandled SQLite readonly-write exception, and `index --dry-run --db file:...immutable=1` remains allowed because dry-run does not mutate the database. Added focused CLI regressions for the absolute/relative read-only `status` remediation paths, the direct `index --db file:...immutable=1` error path, and the `--dry-run` exemption. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Cli/DbPathResolver.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Cli/IndexCommandRunner.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Cli/QueryCommandRunner.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/DbPathResolverTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/IndexCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `README.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #854, #867, #892.
- **Exact graph-supported definition probe no longer crashes on legacy/read-only DBs missing `container_kind` (#493)** — `DbSymbolReader.GetExactGraphSupportedDefinitionLanguage` now routes its C# enum-container filter through the shared `GetSymbolColumnSql("container_kind", "''")` compatibility helper, so the probe stays on the backward-compatible read-schema contract when `TryMigrateForRead()` cannot add the `container_kind` column in place (for example on a read-only filesystem). This keeps exact `inspect`, `references`, `callers`, `callees`, MCP `analyze_symbol`, and the graph-support hints used by `impact` working on older indexes instead of hard-failing with `SQLite Error 1: 'no such column: s.container_kind'`. Added a focused regression that seeds a legacy DB, drops `container_kind`, bypasses read-time migration, and verifies the exact graph-support probe still resolves the symbol language. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbSymbolReader.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/DbReaderTests.cs`. Closes #493.
- **Java `module-info.java` now emits JPMS symbols and keeps filtered repo maps coherent (#305, #889, #898, #899, #911)** — `SymbolExtractor` now indexes Java module declarations as `namespace` symbols and surfaces `requires`, `exports`, `opens`, `uses`, and `provides` directives as `import` rows, including multiline `to` / `with` lists, comment-separated `requires` statements, and Allman brace-line directives, while `RepoMapBuilder` now uses the declared module name for both `module-info.java` and sibling Java source files that belong to the same JPMS module even when `map --path` filters the descriptor file out. `symbols`, `definition`, `outline`, and `map` no longer treat JPMS descriptors as empty or anonymous structure, and `map` now surfaces a coherent module boundary for the whole Java module across both full and path-filtered views. Added focused Java extractor and map regressions and updated the language-pattern reference. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Database/RepoMapBuilder.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Models/QueryResults.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #305, #889, #898, #899, #911.
- **JS/TS no-paren constructor calls now reach the reference graph (#295)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now emits `instantiate` edges for JavaScript / TypeScript zero-argument constructor sites that legally omit `()`, such as `new Foo;`, `new Date;`, qualified targets like `new Demo.Provider;`, and one-level generic TypeScript forms like `new Box<number>;`. Added focused extractor regressions for the reported JS and TS shapes. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `README.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`, `CLAUDE.md`. Closes #295.
- **C# exact `references` now expands multiline `using static` constant-pattern context until the real anchor is visible (#863)** — `DbReader` now grows the read-path context window for C# exact `type_reference` suppression until a `case`/`is` anchor becomes visible instead of stopping after a tiny fixed lookback, so long multiline `switch` constant patterns no longer leak the final `Red:` head in exact JSON or count results. Added focused CLI regressions for the long multiline `case` pattern and its `--count` path. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Closes #863.
- **C# exact qualified constant-pattern rows now stay suppressed end-to-end (#869)** — `ReferenceExtractor` no longer emits phantom `call` rows for same-file enum/member sites such as `case Color.Red or Color.Blue:`, and `DbReader` still keeps the exact-name suppress path pinned to the resolved qualifier instead of unrelated same-name types like `class Red {}`. Added built-CLI regressions that lock exact `references`, `--count`, and `callers` at zero for the reported single-file repro. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Closes #869.
- **C# exact using-static suppress now survives blank/comment separators and long multiline `case` chains (#862, #873)** — `DbReader` no longer relies on a fixed reconstruction window when exact-name C# `using static` suppression checks multiline `case` / `or` chains. It now reconstructs anchor-aware context across blank lines, treats comment-only lines as trivia, and keeps walking backward until it rediscovers the real pattern anchor, so blank-line splits, comment-separated heads, and long multiline constant patterns no longer leak phantom `type_reference` rows in exact `references` queries. Added built-CLI regressions that keep the non-exact ambiguous rows visible while asserting exact `references` and `--count` stay at zero for all three reported shapes. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Closes #862. Closes #873.
- **C# multiline `not` continuation lines now keep real type-pattern heads (#891)** — `ReferenceExtractor` no longer clears pending multiline C# `is` / `case` type-pattern state just because the next continuation line contains only `not`. Valid forms such as `value is` / `not` / `Point` and `case` / `not` / `Point:` now keep their real `type_reference` rows instead of disappearing before the head line is reached. Added extractor and exact-name CLI regressions for both shapes. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Closes #891.
- **C# non-type `case` labels no longer arm multiline type-pattern carry (#857)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now only enters the pending multiline C# `case`-type state when the `case` line actually ends where a later type head can continue. Relational and other non-type labels such as `case > 0:` and `case not > 0:` therefore no longer cause the next-line call token to be mis-recorded as a phantom `type_reference`. Added extractor and CLI regressions that pin the negative `case > 0:` shape alongside the multiline positive cases. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #857.
- **C# multiline type-pattern carry now survives trivia-only separator lines (#850)** — `ReferenceExtractor` no longer flushes pending C# multiline `is` / `case` pattern state just because a structurally masked comment-only line collapses to whitespace, so comment-separated heads such as `value is` + `// comment` + `Point`, `case` + `// comment` + `Point:`, and multiline `using static` constant patterns now keep their `type_reference` rows. Added extractor and CLI regressions for the comment-separated `is`, `case`, and `using static` shapes, and updated the developer guide to document the trivia-only carry boundary. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #850.
- **C# multiline `case` labels now keep cross-line first heads and later logical heads without reviving qualified constant-pattern noise (#843, #747)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now carries C# `case` labels through the same pending type-pattern state machine used by multiline `is` / `as`, so both `case` + next-line first heads (for example `case` / `Point:`) and cross-line logical continuations (for example `case Point or` / `Shape:`) preserve their `type_reference` rows. The new carry path also leaves multiline using-static ambiguous constant heads visible for read-time filtering, while keeping qualified constant/member labels such as `case Color.Red or` / `Color.Blue:` suppressed so no phantom `type_reference` rows come back. Added extractor plus CLI regressions for the multiline `case` type, logical, and constant-pattern shapes, and updated the developer guide to match the new C# pattern boundary. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #843. Closes #747.
- **C# switch-expression arm type patterns now index the arm heads as compile-time dependencies (#732)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now scans C# switch-expression arm heads through the same type-vs-constant discrimination already used for `case` labels, so arm heads such as `Point =>`, `Point or Shape =>`, and generic heads like `Wrapper<Point, Shape> =>` emit `type_reference` rows while constant/member arms such as `Color.Red =>` stay on the non-type path. Added focused extractor and CLI regressions for bare type heads, multi-head logical patterns, generic type heads, and the constant-member control. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #732.
- **C# multi-line expression-bodied members now keep full signatures and same-line terminator-line siblings (#835, #836)** — `SymbolExtractor` now reuses the shared semicolon-terminated declaration extent scan for multi-line expression-bodied C# members, so stored signatures include continuation lines through the terminating `;` instead of truncating at the declaration line. The same extent data now also narrows the #779 continuation-line body guard: later-line field/property candidates remain suppressed while they are still inside the already-emitted member body, but real same-line siblings that start after the terminating `;` on the final line are allowed through again. Added focused extractor plus CLI `symbols` / `outline` regressions for the reported `Match(...) => value is` + `Red; public int X;` shape. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Closes #835. Closes #836.
- **C# multiline constant patterns no longer emit phantom property symbols or lose file-level references (#779)** — `SymbolExtractor` now stops reinterpreting later lines inside an already-emitted C# member body as field/property declarations, so continued constant-pattern heads such as `value is` followed by later-line `Red / or / Red` no longer leak bogus `property Red` symbols into `symbols`, `outline`, or `inspect`. `ReferenceExtractor` also carries C# `is` / `as` pattern heads across line breaks, preserving the real `type_reference` rows for multiline heads instead of dropping them when the first type token starts on the next line. Added focused extractor regressions plus CLI `inspect` / `references` coverage for the reported shape. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Closes #779.
- **JS/TS template-hole line-continuation strings now keep hole state and real calls (#433)** — `StructuralLineMasker` now tracks JavaScript/TypeScript single- and double-quoted strings that continue across physical lines inside `${...}` holes, so a continued string body can no longer close the hole early with `}` or leak phantom references, and the real call that follows the string stays visible to the reference graph. Added JS/TS regression coverage for the continued-string repro. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/StructuralLineMasker.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #433.
- **C# nullable tuple suffixes no longer hide the trailing real reference after parenthesized query clauses (#616)** — the reverse scan that classifies `?` before `select(`/`group(`/`orderby(` as a nullable cast/type-test suffix now also balances tuple parentheses, so legal casts such as `value as (int Left, int Right)?` no longer stop at `)` and wrongly suppress the later `Status.Ready` reference outside the query. Added focused extractor and CLI regressions for the nullable tuple suffix alongside the existing nullable scalar/array coverage. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Closes #616.
- **C# exact `definition` / `inspect` no longer promote interpolated call sites into fake function definitions (#790)** — `SymbolExtractor` now rejects C# symbol candidates whose start column lives inside nested interpolation code instead of root code, so exact `definition` / `inspect` return only the real declaration for `DescribeState`-style methods even when multi-line interpolated log strings contain call-site text that previously stitched into phantom `function` rows. Added extractor and CLI regressions for the reported multi-line interpolation repro. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Closes #790.
- **Zero-result `unused` / `hotspots` now suppress stale SQL trust warnings when the active symbol kind cannot match any SQL symbols (#829)** — zero-result symbol-oriented summaries no longer inherit `sql_graph_contract_ready=false` merely because the workspace contains stale SQL graph rows somewhere else. `unused` / `unused --count`, `hotspots`, grouped hotspots, and the MCP `unused_symbols` / `symbol_hotspots` tools now narrow zero-result SQL trust relevance against the filtered SQL symbol candidate set, so queries such as `--kind interface` or `--kind class` stay clean on mixed C# + SQL indexes when no SQL symbol could possibly contribute. Added focused CLI/MCP regressions for the non-SQL-kind zero-result paths. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbSymbolReader.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Cli/QueryCommandRunner.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Mcp/McpToolHandlers.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/McpServerTests.cs`. Closes #829.
- **CLI `unused --count` zero-result summaries now keep stale SQL trust warnings in mixed SQL-backed scopes (#828)** — `unused --json --count` no longer infers `sql_graph_contract_ready` relevance solely from counted rows, so a zero-result mixed SQL-backed scope now preserves the stale SQL trust fields instead of returning a false-clean summary. Added a focused CLI regression for the zero-result count-only path. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Cli/QueryCommandRunner.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Closes #828.
- **Zero-result SQL-backed dependency/unused summaries no longer drop stale trust warnings (#824)** — zero-result CLI/MCP `deps`, `hotspots`, and `unused` payloads now keep `sql_graph_contract_ready` / `sql_graph_contract_degraded_reason` when the active scope itself is SQL-backed, instead of inferring `Relevant=false` from an empty visible result set. This keeps stale SQL graph-contract DBs from returning falsely clean zero-result dependency or unused answers in JSON/MCP while preserving the existing non-SQL clean paths. Added focused CLI/MCP regressions for zero-result SQL-scope fixtures. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Cli/QueryCommandRunner.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Mcp/McpToolHandlers.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/McpServerTests.cs`. Closes #824.
- **Mixed-language SQL trust-signal narrowing now also stays accurate for CLI `--count` summaries (#822, #823)** — `references` / `callers` / `callees --count` and `unused --count` no longer decide `sql_graph_contract_ready` relevance from a single preview row or from `--lang sql` alone. The count readers now report whether the aggregated scope actually includes any SQL-backed rows/symbols, so mixed C# + SQL counts keep the degraded SQL trust fields whenever SQL contributes to the returned aggregate, while pure non-SQL counts remain clean on the same stale mixed-language index. Added focused CLI regressions for mixed count-only fixtures plus `DbReader` compatibility touch-ups for the updated count summary shape. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbSymbolReader.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Models/QueryResults.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Cli/QueryCommandRunner.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/DbReaderTests.cs`. Closes #822, #823.
- **SQL graph trust signals no longer degrade unrelated non-SQL query results in mixed-language indexes (#818)** — CLI/MCP graph and dependency payloads now emit `sql_graph_contract_ready` / `sql_graph_contract_degraded_reason` only when SQL-backed rows actually contribute to the result or exact-resolution path. Mixed-language indexes that contain stale SQL graph rows no longer mark pure C# / JS / etc. `callers` / `callees` / `references` / `inspect` / `impact` / `deps` / `unused` / `hotspots` output as degraded just because SQL files exist elsewhere in scope. Added focused CLI/MCP regressions for mixed SQL+C# fixtures so stale SQL contract rows stay visible for SQL queries while pure C# queries remain clean. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbSymbolReader.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Models/QueryResults.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Cli/QueryCommandRunner.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Mcp/McpToolHandlers.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/McpServerTests.cs`, `README.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #818.
- **SQL graph readiness now degrades explicitly until unchanged SQL rows are rebuilt under the current contract (#786)** — Added a dedicated `sql_graph_contract_version` stamp plus `sql_graph_contract_ready` / `sql_graph_contract_degraded_reason` status fields so CLI status, index summaries, exact graph queries, `deps`, `unused`, `hotspots`, and `impact`, plus MCP status/dependency/impact tools, all tell users and AI clients when a DB still carries pre-fix SQL graph rows. Normal full scans now force unchanged SQL files to be reindexed when that contract stamp is stale, restoring current SQL call-column semantics before restamping readiness. Added CLI/MCP regressions for degraded status exposure plus the full-scan upgrade path. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbContext.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbWriter.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Models/QueryResults.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Cli/IndexCommandRunner.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Cli/QueryCommandRunner.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Mcp/McpToolHandlers.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/IndexCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/McpServerTests.cs`, `README.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`, `CLAUDE.md`. Closes #786.
- **SQL graph queries now keep schema-qualified readers schema-scoped across exact and non-exact lookups, while definition-oriented readers continue to normalize quoted qualified names consistently (#296, #710, #713, #716, #721, #724, #728, #738, #742, #744, #745, #746, #754, #758, #763, #765, #769, #770, #775, #778, #781, #796, #797, #803)** — SQL definition names remain stored with their schema/package qualifier, but graph/dependency readers now reconstruct each SQL reference identity from the stored source-line context plus recorded call column before comparing it to definitions. That keeps qualified `references` / `callers` / `impact` lookups on the matching schema even when one line contains multiple qualified calls or the lookup is non-exact, while still letting genuinely unqualified SQL source sites fall back to the bare leaf. When a row already has a recorded call column, downstream readers now refuse to whole-line-upgrade that row to a different qualified token later on the line, so comment text, string literals, or a second qualified call no longer make `deps` / `unused` / `hotspots` misattribute the first unqualified call. Aggregate `deps` / `unused` / `hotspots` readers now also reuse the same raw-leaf fallback guard as the exact SQL graph readers, so a bare `EXEC fn_Target` still counts toward `dbo.fn_Target` when no real one-segment `fn_Target` definition exists in scope instead of disappearing from dependency, hotspot, or unused summaries. The SQL no-parens call scanner now also preserves ANSI / PostgreSQL double-quoted call targets such as `CALL "sales"."proc_name"` / `EXEC "dbo"."fn_Target"` instead of stripping them as string literals, so exact/unqualified SQL graph lookups can see those real call edges without emitting phantom qualifier targets. The same SQL no-parens scan now masks earlier same-line string literals and inline block comments with equal-length spaces before matching, so recorded call columns stay aligned with raw source lines and qualified `references` / `callers` still reach `dbo.fn_Target` in shapes such as `SELECT 'prefix'; EXEC dbo.fn_Target;` or `SELECT /*note*/ 1; EXEC dbo.fn_Target;`. Exact SQL graph/dependency readers now also preserve the resolved segment count, so a quoted single identifier containing a dot such as `"sales.fn_Target"` no longer collides with the real qualified name `sales.fn_Target` across exact `references` / `callers` / `impact` and aggregate `deps` / `unused` / `hotspots`. Quoted single-identifier containers that merely contain dots, such as `"sales.Caller"`, also no longer donate a fake schema qualifier when an unqualified `EXEC fn_Target` falls back to the leaf target, so aggregate readers prefer the real exact one-segment target over `sales.fn_Target`. Qualified SQL `callees` queries likewise no longer widen to sibling callers such as `dbo.Caller` when the query is `sales.Caller`, and exact SQL `callees` now normalize bracketed T-SQL caller names such as `[sales].[fn_Target]` before matching so `callees sales.fn_Target --exact` reaches the same outgoing edges as the raw bracketed form. Non-exact CLI JSON `references` / `callers` / `callees` results now keep the SQL graph contract trust fields, while MCP `definition` stops surfacing those graph-contract signals because definition results do not depend on SQL graph readiness. SQL symbol extraction now applies the same shared qualified-identifier pattern across the remaining CREATE / ALTER DDL variants, so supported definitions such as `sales . seq_orders`, `[sales] . [syn_demo]`, and `"sales" . "ext_demo"` also stay fully qualified instead of truncating to the first segment. The same position-aware path still preserves real same-line calls such as `CREATE PROCEDURE sales.fn_Target AS EXEC dbo.fn_Target;`, keeps `deps` / `unused` / `hotspots` aligned with qualified calls, accepts optional whitespace around qualified-name dots, preserves segment-count-aware exact definition matching, and keeps Unicode exact SQL lookups on the folded path. Added DB/CLI/MCP regressions covering non-exact qualified `references` / `callers` / `callees`, same-line multi-call isolation, downstream mixed-line/comment/string isolation, same-line string/block-comment column preservation, exact qualified schema isolation, bracketed exact callee normalization, quoted-definition normalization, double-quoted SQL call targets, quoted-single-identifier exact/aggregate separation, quoted-container leaf fallback, aggregate bare-call fallback, SQL graph trust-field exposure, Unicode exact matching, multi-name exact symbol counts, and spaced-dot SQL symbol extraction. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Cli/QueryCommandRunner.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SqlNameResolver.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbContext.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbSymbolReader.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Mcp/McpToolHandlers.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/DbReaderTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/McpServerTests.cs`, `README.md`. Closes #296, #710, #713, #716, #721, #724, #728, #738, #742, #744, #745, #746, #754, #758, #763, #765, #769, #770, #775, #778, #781, #796, #797, #803.
- **JavaScript / TypeScript symbol extraction: class-field arrows, top-level generators, and object-literal method shorthand (#212)** — The JS/TS extractor now emits `function` symbols for three constructs that previously fell through to the field-initializer state machine or missed the top-level regex entirely: (1) class-field arrow properties such as `handleClick = () => { }` and the TypeScript annotated form `handleClick = (): void => { }` (including TS generic arrow fields like `transform = <T>(x: T): T => { ... }`), which are captured by a new arrow-specific header parser that walks optional visibility / field-type annotations, the `=` assignment, optional `async`, generic parameters, parameter list or single identifier, optional return-type annotation, and the `=> {` brace-opening; (2) top-level generator functions `function* gen()` / `async function* gen()` — the top-level regex now allows `function\s*\*` between `function` and the name; (3) object-literal method shorthand inside `const obj = { ... }`, `let obj = { ... }`, `var obj = { ... }`, `export const obj = { ... }`, `module.exports = { ... }`, `module.exports.name = { ... }`, and `exports.name = { ... }` — a new object-literal scan-target collector routes these through the existing class-body scanner so `get foo()`, `set foo(v)`, `*bar()`, `async baz()`, and plain `qux()` shorthand members surface as function symbols with `container_kind="object"` and `container_name` set to the binding alias. Plain value fields (`value = 42`, `count: 42`, `items = [1, 2, 3]`) remain correctly classified as non-functions. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`.
- **C# const fields with tuple / nullable / array / qualified return types no longer disappear from symbol extraction (#346)** — `SymbolExtractor` now reuses the shared `CSharpTypePattern` (which understands balanced tuple parentheses, nullable suffixes, array ranks, and qualified names) on the C# `const` field row instead of a hand-rolled `[\w@?.<>\[\],:]+` character class. This keeps declarations such as `public const (int, int) Pair = (1, 2);`, `const (int a, int b) NamedPair = (3, 4);`, `const (int, int)? MaybePair = null;`, `const (int, int)[] PairArray = ...;`, and `const global::System.Int32 Qualified = 42;` visible in `symbols`, `definition`, and `outline` while the matching phantom `function const` guard from #496 still holds. Added a focused regression that locks the five tuple-return shapes and the baseline `int` control. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #346.
- **C# nested `new enum` declarations are no longer dropped by symbol extraction (#353)** — `SymbolExtractor` now accepts the `new` member-hiding modifier in `CSharpEnumDeclarationRegex` alongside the existing `file` modifier, so nested `public new enum Kind { A }` / `new public enum KindReversed { A }` / `public new enum KindByte : byte { A }` forms inside derived types are captured as `enum` symbols instead of silently disappearing from `symbols`, `definition`, and `outline`. Added focused regressions for the enum fix and for the already-supported `new delegate` companion, pinning both `public new delegate` and reversed `new public delegate` modifier orders. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #353.
- **Strengthened #362 regression coverage for C# `new Qualified.Type()` phantom-method shapes** — The core fix (excluding `new` from the explicit-interface return-type slot) shipped in v1.13.0. This adds a broader end-to-end regression fixture (`Svc` with `ChainedNew` / `DiscardNew` / `UseNew` / `BraceInitNew` methods emitting `new System.Text.StringBuilder().Append(...).ToString()`, `_ = new System.Text.RegularExpressions.Regex(...)`, `new System.Net.Http.HttpClient().Dispose()`, and brace-initializer `new Outer.Inner { A = 1 }.Consume()` forms, plus an `Outer.Inner.Consume()` container chain and a sibling `Consumer : System.IDisposable` with a real `void System.IDisposable.Dispose() { }`) and pins the full five-axis `Outer` -> `Inner` -> `Consume` chain (total `Outer`=1, total `Inner`=1, `Inner` under `Outer`=1, total `Consume`=1, `Consume` under `Inner`=1) alongside a kind-agnostic `Name == "new"` absence guard, so future widening of the explicit-interface pattern cannot silently regress the dot-chain-qualifier / brace-initializer / discard / chained-call shapes. Affected: `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Re-runs #362.
- **C# body-less same-line members no longer absorb the next file line's brace range (#515)** — `SymbolExtractor.FindCSharpBraceRange` now clamps body-less function-like declarations (`void M();`, ctor signatures, etc.) at the in-line `;` once a parameter list `(` has been seen, instead of relying solely on the trailing `EndsWith(';')` fallback. That fallback never fired for same-line shapes whose physical line ends with the enclosing type's `}` (`{ int P { get; } void M(); }` or even single-member `{ void M(); }`), so the scanner kept reading into the next file line and attributed the next class's `{ ... }` body range — and container — to the body-less symbol. The new clamp uses a `sawOpenParen` guard so it stays inert when `absoluteStartColumn` arrives in collapsed-generic / attribute-stripped match-line space and slides the scan onto an earlier sibling's `;` (e.g. `event ... E;` ahead of a same-line `class Wrapped<T>`). Added a focused regression that pins range / body / container metadata for the property→method, single-member, and method→property orderings plus the `property + event` baseline. Verified on the locally built binary against the issue repros: M now reports `end_line=1` with null body for `interface I { void M(); }` and `end_line=2` for `interface J { int P { get; } void M(); }`. Also confirmed that the originally-reported #514 generic-event/interface/delegate semicolon loss no longer reproduces (the trailing `;` is preserved by the existing collapsed-to-raw signature translator), so this commit also closes #514. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`, `CHANGELOG.md`. Closes #514, #515.
- **C# top-level statement callers now surface as a synthetic `<top-level>` graph caller (#242)** — `DbReader` no longer drops C# call-graph rows whose `symbol_references.container_name` is null because the call site lives in top-level statements. `callers`, `callers --count`, `impact`, `inspect`, and other caller-backed read paths now surface those rows as `function <top-level>` instead of returning a false zero-caller result for `Program.cs`-style entrypoints. Added focused DB and CLI regressions for the top-level `Run();` repro while preserving the existing outline contract that executable top-level bodies are not indexed as symbols. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/DbReaderTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `CHANGELOG.md`. Closes #242.
- **Java Allman-style compact constructors are indexed again (#791)** — `SymbolExtractor` now accepts compact-constructor headers whose opening `{` moves to the next physical line, so records such as `public record Sample(int value)\n{\n    public Sample\n    {\n        ...\n    }\n}` no longer lose the constructor symbol. Added a focused Java extractor regression that pins the Allman-style constructor symbol, body range, and record component. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #791.
- **Java same-line body-less members now keep later same-line siblings under the same container (#788)** — `SymbolExtractor` now restarts Java body-less declaration scanning from the next sibling after a top-level `;` and clamps same-line Java signatures to that statement terminator. This keeps later members such as `int age();` in `@interface Tags { String[] value(); int age(); } class C {}` indexed as members of `Tags` instead of swallowing `} class C {}` into the signature or dropping the sibling entirely. Added a focused Java extractor regression that pins both member signatures and the trailing same-line `class C`. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #788.
- **Comma-separated SQL temp names no longer disappear into MySQL-style `# comment` stripping (#789)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now iterates every captured temp target in repeated `TRUNCATE TABLE #a, #b` lists and recognizes comma-separated SQL identifier lists before a `#temp` as source/target continuations rather than `# comment` tails. This keeps later temp names visible in both `TRUNCATE TABLE #a, #b; SELECT * FROM #b;` and `DELETE FROM audit_log USING #a, #b` without regressing real MySQL hash comments. Added focused extractor regressions for both the truncation-establish path and the `DELETE ... USING` source list. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #789.
- **C# exact `references` with `using static` now page past large suppressed prefixes without unstable same-line ordering (#735)** — `DbReader` now keeps the `using static` constant-pattern suppression on the exact C# read path while switching the filtered search loop from restart-with-larger-limit to `OFFSET`-based paging with expanding page sizes and a total-order exact-reference sort (`line`, `column_number`, `reference_kind`, `symbol_name`), so a real later hit is no longer lost after 65,536 suppressed enum-member pattern rows or at same-line page boundaries. Added `DbReader` regressions that seed 65,537 suppressed rows ahead of a later real call and pin same-line exact-reference ordering by column. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/DbReaderTests.cs`. Closes #735.
- **Java same-line `@interface` members and annotated record headers keep correct body ranges (#784)** — `SymbolExtractor` now keeps Java same-line nested-member scanning on its Java-specific brace path instead of reusing the C# same-line brace-end scanner, treats top-level `;` as a body-less Java declaration terminator even when a later sibling `}` appears on the same line, and ignores annotation-array braces while locating the first real class/record body opener for compact-constructor discovery. This prevents `@interface Tags { String[] value(); } class C {}` from stretching `value()` into the next declaration and keeps compact record constructors indexed for headers such as `record Sample(@Tags({"a", "b"}) int value) { public Sample { ... } }`. Added focused Java extractor regressions for same-line `@interface` members, enum anonymous-member bodies, and annotation-array record headers. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #784.
- **SQL `TRUNCATE TABLE #temp` now establishes later temp reads (#768)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now treats temp-table truncation as an establishing write in the same temp-object gating path used by `INSERT` / `UPDATE` / `DELETE` / `SELECT ... INTO` / `CREATE TABLE`, so `TRUNCATE TABLE #temp; SELECT * FROM #temp;` no longer drops the later read. Added focused extractor and CLI regressions for both direct and line-end-comment truncation forms. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #768.
- **SQL bare identifiers keep non-ASCII unquoted names while also allowing `$` (#764)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now keeps the SQL bare-identifier contract Unicode-aware while still allowing PostgreSQL-style `$` characters, so unquoted names such as `ユーザー` continue to index on source/target/proc-call paths instead of disappearing from exact-name graph queries. Added focused extractor and CLI regressions that lock both `reference` and `call` counts for non-ASCII SQL identifiers. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #764.
- **SQL line-end target-prefix carry now covers general target forms, and bare `$` identifiers stay whole on source/target paths (#726, #759)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now keeps unfinished target-introducer prefixes such as `INSERT INTO -- comment`, `UPDATE -- comment`, `DELETE FROM -- comment`, `TRUNCATE TABLE -- comment`, and `CREATE TABLE -- comment` on the same SQL statement path, so the next-line object name is still indexed as a `reference`, temp-target establishment still reaches later reads, and `INSERT INTO ... (` does not regress into a phantom `call` on the target identifier. The shared bare SQL identifier contract now also accepts PostgreSQL-style `$` characters (for example `my$table`) across SQL source/target readers, target-call suppression, and exact-name lookups, preventing truncation to the prefix before `$`. Added focused extractor and CLI regressions for both behaviors. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #726, #759.
- **SQL source/target references now cover ordinary identifiers and temp tables without leaking `SELECT ... INTO` variables, overloaded `USING` clauses, or SQL string/comment bodies into the graph (#284, #638, #639, #648, #649, #653, #657, #660, #664, #665, #679, #681, #684, #689, #690, #694, #695, #696, #697, #698, #707, #708, #711, #712, #718, #729, #739, #740, #741, #750, #752, #753)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now emits SQL `reference` rows for named source/target forms such as `FROM`, `JOIN`, `INSERT INTO`, `UPDATE`, `TRUNCATE TABLE`, `DELETE FROM`, `DELETE ... USING`, `MERGE [INTO]`, and `MERGE ... USING`, recognizes T-SQL `#temp` / `##temp` identifiers in target paths plus later statement-order reads of temp objects established by earlier `INSERT INTO #temp`, `INSERT TOP (...) INTO #temp`, `UPDATE #temp`, `DELETE FROM #temp`, `SELECT ... INTO #temp`, multiline `SELECT ... INTO #temp`, `CREATE TABLE #temp`, and `MERGE [INTO] #temp` statements, keeps `EXEC`/`CALL` plus table-valued functions on the `call` path (including bracketed/backtick/double-quoted `FROM ... fn(...)` shapes), skips modifier keywords such as `SELECT|INSERT|UPDATE|MERGE|DELETE TOP (...)`, `ONLY`, and `LATERAL` before resolving the real source/target object, and advances temp state across both same-line `;`-separated statements and later semicolon-less statement boundaries including top-level `SET`, `DECLARE`, `DO`, and `BEGIN`. It skips the `MERGE ... WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE SET ...` action-clause false positive while keeping quoted `[SET]` targets valid, suppresses the old `INSERT INTO ... (` phantom `call` edge plus `SELECT|INSERT|UPDATE|MERGE|DELETE TOP (...)` phantom `call TOP`, no longer treats unrelated PostgreSQL `USING` clauses such as `CREATE INDEX ... USING btree (...)` as source/call edges, keeps real SQL expression calls such as `ALTER TABLE ... USING lower(name)` on the `call` path, keeps SQL Server `MERGE target USING source` forms with or without optional `INTO` including temp-table targets, keeps same-line and multiline SQL Server target hints such as `MERGE INTO audit_log WITH (INDEX(ix), HOLDLOCK) AS t USING staging_log ...`, `MERGE INTO #audit_log` followed by a next-line `WITH (...) AS t`, and unfinished temp/source/hint prefixes split by a line-end `-- comment` such as `SELECT ... INTO -- comment`, `DELETE ... USING src, -- comment`, and `MERGE ... WITH (INDEX(...), -- comment` on the same SQL continuation path, preserves multiline `DELETE ... USING` / `MERGE ... USING` source continuations even when the previous line ended with a `-- comment`, restores PostgreSQL `DELETE ... USING` source references without reopening the DDL `USING` false positives, handles `TRUNCATE TABLE ONLY public.users` plus comma-separated target lists like `TRUNCATE TABLE audit_log, archived_log` as real targets instead of phantom `ONLY` or missing later targets, and keeps those `TRUNCATE` / `DELETE ... USING` paths canonicalized to the terminal object name only so qualified or quoted-qualified forms do not emit duplicate or mangled exact-name graph rows. It treats double-quoted SQL text as object names only when the introducer keyword itself sits outside the quoted region so dynamic SQL strings no longer leak phantom `reference users` or falsely establish later `#temp` reads, blanks multiline single-quoted string bodies plus multiline `/* ... */` comments and PostgreSQL `$$...$$` / `$tag$...$tag$` bodies before source/target scanning so non-code SQL text cannot leak phantom `reference phantom` rows or phantom temp establishment, closes same-line dollar-quoted bodies at the first real delimiter so later same-line statements like `...$$; SELECT * FROM users; ...` are still scanned, preserves MySQL/MariaDB backslash-escaped single quotes inside SQL string scanning so `\'` literals also stay opaque, and still strips MySQL/MariaDB `# comment` tails so `CALL #commented_out` / `FROM #commented_source` do not leak phantom edges. Added focused SQL regression fixtures covering CTE reuse, view/table references, stored procedures, TVFs, quoted TVFs, temp tables, `MERGE ... USING`, optional-`INTO` SQL Server `MERGE` forms, `MERGE [INTO] #temp` forms, `DELETE ... USING`, overloaded-`USING` DDL controls, real `USING <expr>` call preservation, `MERGE ... USING` target hints, multiline temp-merge target hints, unfinished line-end comment continuations, `MERGE ... UPDATE SET` controls, same-line temp statement ordering, semicolon-less temp statement boundaries, `SET` / `DECLARE` temp reads, SQL source/target modifiers, `TRUNCATE TABLE ONLY` / multi-target lists, qualified / quoted-qualified canonicalization guards, dynamic-SQL double-quote guards, dynamic-SQL temp-establishment guards, multiline single-quoted-string guards, multiline block-comment guards, PostgreSQL dollar-quoted-body guards, same-line dollar-quoted-body boundaries, escaped single-quote guards, multiline temp-table materialization, ANSI quoted identifiers, MySQL `# comment` controls, and the `SELECT ...
