INTO variable` guard, and documented the narrowed SQL graph pattern in the developer guide. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #284, #638, #639, #648, #649, #653, #657, #660, #664, #665, #679, #681, #684, #689, #690, #694, #695, #696, #697, #698, #707, #708, #711, #712, #718, #729, #741, #750, #752, #753.
- **SQL semicolon-less temp-state flushing now recognizes top-level `IF` and `WHILE` boundaries (#757)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now treats top-level `IF` / `WHILE` as semicolon-less SQL statement starts for temp-object carry flushing, so `SELECT ... INTO #temp` immediately followed by `IF ... SELECT * FROM #temp` or `WHILE ... SELECT * FROM #temp` preserves the later `#temp` read instead of keeping only the establish-side target reference. Added focused extractor and CLI regressions for both control-flow forms. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #757.
- **C# methods with generic attributes on type parameters no longer disappear from symbol extraction (#347)** — `SymbolExtractor` now shares a nested-angle-aware C# method type-parameter matcher across the regular and explicit-interface method rows, so declarations such as `public void M<[GenAttr<int>] U>(U u)`, `public void N<[GenAttr<int>, GenAttr<string>] U>(U u)`, `public void P<[GenAttr<(int, int)>] U>(U u)`, and `void IFoo.Run<[GenAttr<int>] T>(T value)` remain visible in `symbols`, `definition`, and `outline` instead of being silently dropped by a shallow `<[^>]+>` match. Added focused regression coverage for ordinary and explicit-interface methods. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #347.
- **C# phantom local `const` and qualified call-site function symbols are now filtered from symbol extraction (#496)** — `SymbolExtractor` now applies the existing column-aware C# type-body gate to field-like `function` rows (`const` / `static readonly`) so local declarations such as `const string content = "hello";` no longer leak into `symbols`, `definition`, or `outline`. It also rejects C# declaration candidates whose captured return-type fragment ends in an operator/contextual suffix, preventing qualified call arguments like `elapsed < TimeSpan.FromSeconds(10)` from surfacing phantom `function FromSeconds` rows. Added a focused regression that locks both repros. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #496.
- **C# verbatim-identifier return types no longer get dropped by the #496 suffix guard (#782)** — `HasInvalidCSharpReturnTypeSuffix` now recognizes real C# verbatim identifiers before rejecting contextual-keyword tails, so declarations such as `public @new Make() => new @new();` remain visible in `symbols` and `outline` while the `elapsed < TimeSpan.FromSeconds(...)` phantom-call-site filter stays intact. Added a focused regression for the verbatim return-type repro. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #782.
- **C# contextual-keyword type names like `await` / `yield` no longer get dropped by the #496 suffix guard (#785)** — `HasInvalidCSharpReturnTypeSuffix` now keeps legal contextual-identifier type names while still rejecting truly invalid statement/operator tails, so members such as `public await MakeAwait()` and `public yield MakeYield()` remain visible in `symbols`, `definition`, and `outline`. Added a focused regression covering both contextual-keyword return types. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #785.
- **C# parenthesized LINQ clause guard no longer mistakes local `const` identifiers for cast targets (#624)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now treats typed local `const` declarations as in-scope value names for the cast-close disambiguation path, so parenthesized `orderby` expressions such as `(READY)` and `(Select)` no longer suppress the real trailing `select(...)` clause solely because the identifier casing looks type-like. Real casts like `(CustomType)select(items)` still keep the clause blocked. Added focused extractor and CLI `references --exact-name --json` regressions for the uppercase-constant repro, and the existing keyword-named-constant regression now passes against the built binary again. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `CHANGELOG.md`. Closes #624.
- **Installer jq-version detection test now uses a real PATH stub instead of a bash function shim (#774)** — `InstallScriptTests.ResolveVersion_UsesJqWhenAvailable` now creates an executable `jq` stub under a temporary directory and prepends that directory to `PATH`, so CI reliably exercises the `command -v jq` branch without depending on bash function lookup details or the runner's preinstalled tools. This keeps the installer's jq-preferring path covered while avoiding spurious stderr on GitHub Actions. Affected: `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/InstallScriptTests.cs`, `CHANGELOG.md`. Closes #774.
- **Java same-line annotations, enum-constant body overrides, and record compact constructors now extract correctly (#221, #751, #755)** — `SymbolExtractor` now strips leading same-line Java annotations with the existing lexer-aware scanner instead of a flat `[^)]*` regex prefix, so declarations such as `@Label(")") public int broken()`, `@SuppressWarnings({"unchecked"}) public int first() { ... }`, and annotated `record` headers survive string/comment/paren/brace edge cases. The extractor also emits record compact constructors in both same-line (`public Range { ... }`) and Allman-style (`public Range` followed by next-line `{`) layouts, preserves anonymous enum-member body ranges so overrides like `ADD { @Override public int apply(...) { ... } }` attach to the enum constant container, keeps same-line Java brace-bodied siblings separate (`int first() { ... } int second() { ... }`) instead of swallowing later methods into the first signature, and now restarts same-line Java scanning after enum constants so methods inside `A { @Override int f() { ... } int g() { ... } }` also reach the symbol table. Added focused Java regressions for the reported repros plus the enum-member body range contract. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #221. Closes #751. Closes #755.
- **C# `unused` now reports truly unused enum members instead of degrading the result (#488)** — `unused`, `unused --count`, and MCP `unused_symbols` no longer exclude C# enum-member rows once qualified enum-member access edges are indexed, and the stale `graph_degraded=true` / `unsupported_symbol_kind=enum_member` metadata has been removed. Added DB, CLI, and MCP regressions that keep referenced enum members out while returning genuinely unused members such as `Green`. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbSymbolReader.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Cli/QueryCommandRunner.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Mcp/McpToolHandlers.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/DbReaderTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/McpServerTests.cs`. Closes #488.
- **Test suite no longer serializes every class assembly-wide (#528)** — Removed the assembly-level xUnit `DisableTestParallelization` gate so independent test classes can run concurrently, while keeping process-global hazards serialized by assigning environment-variable and debug-console tests to the existing non-parallel `SQLite pool sensitive` collection. Updated the testing guide to document the new parallel-by-default contract plus the required collection/console-lock rules. Affected: `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/TestAssembly.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/GitHubIssueReporterTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/DbDebugTests.cs`, `TESTING_GUIDE.md`. Closes #528.
- **C# XML-doc `cref` extraction no longer indexes plain `//` comments as real type dependencies (#646)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now requires an actual C# XML-doc comment line before it emits `type_reference` edges from `cref="..."`, so comment text like `// <see cref="Foo"/>` no longer pollutes `references` / `impact` with false compile-time dependencies while real `/// <see cref="Foo"/>` docs continue to work. Added a focused regression test that locks the plain-comment repro. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`, `CLAUDE.md`. Closes #646.
- **C# `using static` inherited nested-type rescue now canonicalizes constructed generic base type aliases (#734)** — `DbReader` now re-normalizes alias-expanded base references before resolving the containing type, so `references Red --exact-name --lang csharp` keeps inherited nested-type pattern dependencies visible for both local and `global using` aliases such as `AliasBase = Probe.Base<int>`. Added focused CLI regressions for payload and `--count` coverage across local/global constructed-generic alias repros. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #734.
- **C# `using static` inherited nested-type rescue now resolves base classes through active type and namespace aliases (#733)** — `DbReader` now normalizes derived-type base references through active/local and project-wide `using Alias = ...;` mappings before walking the inherited nested-type rescue chain, so `references Red --exact-name --lang csharp` keeps real derived-class pattern dependencies visible for both `class Derived : BaseAlias` and `class Derived : NsAlias.Base` when `Red` is inherited from the aliased base class. Added focused CLI regressions for both payload and `--count` coverage across type-alias and namespace-alias repros. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #733.
- **C# `using static` constant-pattern suppression now keeps multiline logical-pattern heads suppressed (#731)** — `DbReader` now reconstructs a small indexed multi-line context window before running the read-path `is` / `case` anchor scan, so `references Red --exact-name --lang csharp` and `--count` no longer leak phantom `type_reference` rows from `value is` patterns where the imported constant heads (`Red or Blue`) continue on the next line. Added focused CLI regressions that pin both `Red` and `Blue` on the multiline logical-pattern repro. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #731.
- **C# `using static` inherited nested-type rescue no longer treats implemented interfaces as base-class scope (#730)** — `DbReader` now follows inherited nested-type rescue only through real C# base-class chains, so `references Red --exact-name --lang csharp` no longer reports false-positive `type_reference` rows for `value is Red` / `typeof(Red)` when `Red` exists only as an implemented interface nested type. Added focused CLI regressions for both the result payload and `--count` path. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #730.
- **C# `using static` same-name type rescue now keeps inherited protected nested-type patterns visible (#725)** — `DbReader` now treats base-class containing types as rescue candidates when the current C# scope is inside a derived type, so `references Red --exact-name --lang csharp` no longer suppresses real pattern-site dependencies such as `value is Red` when `Red` is a protected nested type inherited from a base class. Added focused CLI regressions for the inherited protected nested-type repro and a derived-class constant-only control. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #725.
- **C# `using static` constant-pattern suppression now keeps real same-name type references visible while still dropping imported constant heads (#682, #686, #692, #702, #703, #704, #705, #709, #717, #719)** — `DbReader` now filters C# pattern-position `type_reference` results through active `using static` imports plus the indexed enum/const-member containers, but it first checks the current C# scope for unqualified same-name type candidates so real type references such as `value is Red` (when `Red` is an actual in-scope type) are not suppressed. The read-path suppression now also consults the unclamped raw line text instead of the display-width-limited preview, so `references --max-line-width` cannot accidentally resurrect phantom matches that the full-width query correctly suppresses. Project-wide `global using static ...;` and `global using Namespace;` directives declared in other files are now included in the active import set for every C# file, while `using Alias = ...;` directives are no longer misread as namespace imports that rescue unrelated same-name types. Active C# type aliases now also block this suppression path, so `using Red = Probe.Real.Red;` keeps `value is Red` visible even when `using static` imported a constant named `Red`. The same-name type rescue now also respects C# `file` visibility, so a file-local `file class Red` in another file cannot make `value is Red` look like a real type dependency outside that file. Same-file `using Namespace;` imports likewise rescue real type heads, and the extractor now leaves ambiguous unqualified `using static` pattern heads in the DB for the workspace-aware read path instead of trying to pre-suppress them with file-local visibility only. That keeps fresh indexes correct when the real same-name type is declared in another file in the same namespace. Pattern-head detection on the read path now tokenizes backward through whitespace and block comments around `is` / `case`, so tabs and trivia-bearing forms like `value is/*comment*/Red` and `case\tRed:` stay suppressed instead of leaking phantom `type_reference` rows. `references --count` on this suppress path now streams raw rows instead of materializing every `ReferenceResult`, so count-only queries no longer pay for preview formatting. Cross-file constant/member cases such as `using static Probe.Color; value is Red or Blue;`, `case Red:`, and `case Red or Blue:` still stop surfacing phantom `type_reference` rows for `Red` / `Blue`, while genuine type heads like `Point` in `case Red or Point:`, same-file namespace-imported `Red`, and cross-file same-namespace `typeof(Red)` / `value is Red` remain visible. Added focused extractor regression coverage for qualified-vs-ambiguous logical-pattern syntax, CLI exact-name / `--count` regressions for the cross-file repros, and same-name real-type regressions that pin file-scoped-namespace, nested-type, same-file `using` namespace imports, same-namespace multi-file rescue, `global using`, alias-scope recovery, file-local visibility, and trivia-bearing constant patterns. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #682. Closes #686. Closes #692. Closes #702. Closes #703. Closes #704. Closes #705. Closes #709. Closes #717. Closes #719.
- **C# XML-doc `cref` extraction no longer leaks plain `//` or `////` comments as compile-time references (#723)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now gates `EmitCSharpDocCrefReferences(...)` on a real XML doc line check instead of merely looking for `cref="` anywhere in the raw source line. Ordinary comments such as `// <see cref="Foo"/>` and `//// <see cref="Foo"/>` no longer emit phantom `type_reference Foo` rows, while genuine `/// <see cref="Foo"/>` lines keep their documented-member container behavior. Added focused extractor regressions for plain-comment and four-slash controls alongside the existing real-doc control. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #723.
- **C# XML-doc `cref` extraction now keeps declaration-attached `/** ... */` documentation comments after the #723 plain-comment guard, without leaking post-`*/` same-line raw-string/code `cref` text, indexing ordinary block comments, misclassifying multiline string content as doc comments, letting method-body doc comments attach to the next declaration, dropping same-line declarations after `*/`, or shifting reference columns left on indented doc lines (#736, #831, #837, #839, #842, #846, #847)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now computes the effective doc-comment slice for each C# XML-doc line and runs `CSharpDocCrefRegex` only against that slice, while preserving `symbol_references.column` relative to the original physical source line. Real declaration-attached `/** ... */` documentation comments keep contributing dependency edges, including declarations that begin later on the same physical line after the closing `*/`; ordinary `//` / `////` comments, non-documenting block comments, and multiline raw/verbatim string content that merely starts with `/**` stay excluded; any `cref` text that appears after the closing `*/` on the same physical line is ignored; and method-body `///` / `/** ... */` comments no longer inherit the next later declaration as their documented container. Added focused extractor regressions for the `/** ... <see cref="Foo"/> ... */` repro, the mixed same-line raw-string follow-up, the same-line declaration-after-`*/` case, both `///` / `/** ... */` column-location contracts, multiline raw/verbatim string false-positive controls, and method-body negative controls for `///` / `/** ... */` even when a later declaration exists, and synchronized `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md` plus `CLAUDE.md` with the final contract. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`. Closes #736. Closes #831. Closes #837. Closes #839. Closes #842. Closes #846. Closes #847.
- **C# XML-doc `cref` extraction no longer lets field-initializer lambda bodies document later members (#853)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now only lets XML-doc comments attach forward when the comment sits at namespace/type declaration depth, not merely anywhere inside a class-like container. That keeps `///` and `/** ... */` comments inside field-initializer lambdas and other nested executable blocks from leaking phantom `type_reference` rows onto later members such as `void Later() {}`. Added focused extractor regressions for both triple-slash and delimited XML-doc comments inside field-initializer lambdas. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `CHANGELOG.md`. Closes #853.
- **C# XML-doc `cref` extraction no longer lets brace-free initializer continuations document later members (#868)** — The C# XML-doc forward-attachment guard now also rejects brace-free executable continuations at type scope, not just nested `{ ... }` blocks. Multiline field/property initializers and brace-free expression lambdas therefore stop leaking `///` / `/** ... */` `cref` rows onto later members such as `void Later() {}`. Added focused extractor regressions for brace-free field-initializer call arguments and brace-free expression lambdas in both triple-slash and delimited forms. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `CHANGELOG.md`. Closes #868.
- **C# XML-doc `cref` extraction no longer lets top-level statements separate a doc comment from a later declaration (#877)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now refuses forward XML-doc attachment when any non-attribute executable code sits between the doc comment and the candidate declaration, which closes the top-level-statement gap where `/// <see cref="Foo"/>` or `/** ... */` above `System.Console.WriteLine(1);` could still leak a phantom `type_reference Foo` onto a later local function or type declaration. Added focused extractor regressions for triple-slash and delimited top-level-statement controls plus a later-type control. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`, `CLAUDE.md`. Closes #877.
- **C# XML-doc `cref` extraction no longer skips past same-line non-target code after `*/` (#887)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now blocks same-line forward attachment when the text after a delimited XML-doc close contains real code or a non-symbol declaration instead of only attributes leading into the documented declaration. This closes the remaining false positive where `/** ... */ string text = "";` on one line could still attach the `cref` to a later `void Run() {}` on the next line, while keeping the valid `/** ... */ [Attr]` then next-line declaration shape working. Added focused extractor regressions for both the negative field-then-method repro and the positive attribute-then-method control. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`, `CLAUDE.md`. Closes #887.
- **C# verbatim type names in pattern/type-keyword references no longer hang or disappear (#677)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now treats `@not` / `@default` as legal escaped identifiers in C# type-pattern and type-keyword scanners instead of collapsing them into bare pattern tokens or stalling on the leading `@`. `value is @not`, `case @default:`, and `typeof(@default)` now emit normalized `type_reference` rows (`not` / `default`) while bare constant patterns such as `is not null` and `case default:` still stay filtered. Added focused extractor and CLI exact-name regressions for both escaped-type and bare-token control cases. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #677.
- **C# type-test references now distinguish `is not` patterns from constant `case Enum.Member` labels without dropping recursive/logical/positional `case Type ...` patterns (#645, #647, #661, #666, #668, #669, #670, #671, #672, #673, #674, #675)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now splits C# type-test handling between `is` / `is not` / `as` expressions and a small structural `case` pattern scanner. This restores the real `Foo` dependency for `x is not Foo`, suppresses phantom constant-pattern rows such as `type_reference not` / `type_reference null`, stops `value is Color.Red or Color.Blue`, `case Color.Red:`, `case Namespace.Color.Red:`, `case Color.Red or Namespace.Color.Blue:`, and `case ErrorCodes.NotFound or ErrorCodes.Forbidden:` from being misclassified as compile-time type dependencies, and still keeps legitimate `case Point:`, `case Point when value != null:`, `case ILogger logger:`, `case Point { X: 0, Y: 0 }:`, `case Point(var x, var y):`, `case Point or null:`, `case not Point:`, `case Probe.Point or null:`, `case not Probe.Point:`, `case global::Probe.Point or null:`, `case N2.Color.Red r:`, `case Outer.Red or Outer.Blue:`, `case Point or Shape:`, `case Color.Red or Point:`, and `case Foo @or:` type references intact, including later genuine heads inside logical patterns. Added focused extractor regressions plus CLI-level multi-file and mixed-logical-pattern regressions for the reported issue repros and constant-pattern / logical-pattern / verbatim-designator / nested-type follow-ups. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #645, #647, #661, #666, #668, #669, #670, #671, #672, #673, #674, #675.
- **C# carried multiline string continuations no longer hide later same-line type declarations (#630, #633)** — `SymbolExtractor` now skips the top-level `"` / `;` / `}` tokens that can appear before the next real C# declaration when a physical line starts by closing a carried verbatim/raw string or a same-line container. The carried-state same-line restart fix now also flows through accessor-event sibling recovery, so `event E { add { } remove { } }` no longer blocks later nested/outer type siblings on the same close-line. Added focused extractor regressions for carried verbatim-string and raw-string continuation lines, including accessor-event variants. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #630, #633.
- **C# same-line nested type signatures no longer absorb later outer siblings (#635)** — `SymbolExtractor` now resolves same-line C# brace-bodied signature endpoints in the carried lexical-state collapsed line domain before translating back to raw columns, so continuation-line close tokens no longer make a nested type such as `Child` swallow the later outer sibling `OuterChild` into its stored signature. The carried verbatim/raw continuation regressions now also assert the exact `Child` / `OuterChild` signatures. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #635.
- **C#/Java type-position identifiers now produce `type_reference` edges (#256)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now indexes C#/Java base lists, declaration types, parameter/return types, generic constraints, Java `throws`, C# `is` / `as`, Java `instanceof`, and C# XML-doc `cref` sites as compile-time dependency edges. This lets `references` and `impact` see rename-sensitive type dependencies such as `class Derived : Base, ILogger`, `Base parent`, `List<ILogger>`, `where T : ILogger`, `throws IOException`, and `<see cref="Base.Do"/>` without polluting the default `callers` / `callees` dynamic call graph. Added focused extractor regressions for the C# and Java issue repros and updated the developer/AI guidance docs to document the expanded `type_reference` contract. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`, `CLAUDE.md`. Closes #256.
- **C# explicit-interface events now keep the trailing event name instead of disappearing from the index (#351)** — `SymbolExtractor` now has a dedicated C# explicit-interface event row, so declarations such as `event EventHandler IFoo.Evt { add { } remove { } }` are indexed as `Evt` under the implementing container instead of being omitted from `symbols`, `definition`, and `outline`. The dedicated row shares the existing explicit-interface qualifier contract, so same-line and next-line accessor blocks and generic interface qualifiers stay aligned with the method/property explicit-member paths. Added focused extractor regression coverage for multiline accessor blocks, same-line accessor blocks, generic qualifiers, and phantom-name guards. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #351.
- **Empty same-line C# nested type bodies no longer swallow later sibling types or closing-line outer properties (#585)** — `SymbolExtractor` now refuses to treat an empty compact class-like body as an internal restart target and reopens the full same-line pattern list for later siblings after compact class-like members, so fixtures such as `public interface I<T1, T2> { } public class Sibling { }` and `public class Child { } } public int P { get; set; }` still expose the later sibling declaration instead of stopping at the nested type's empty body. Added focused extractor regressions for the later-sibling-type and later-outer-property shapes. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #585.
- **C# verbatim identifiers now survive symbol and reference extraction (#367, #626, #627, #628)** — `SymbolExtractor` now accepts `@`-prefixed C# declaration names across the affected declaration rows and normalizes stored symbol names to their semantic identifier (`@class` → `class`), including qualified namespace/import/container names such as `Outer.@class` and conversion-operator target types such as `implicit operator List<@class>`. `ReferenceExtractor` now accepts verbatim call / constructor / parenless-initializer targets without letting keyword ignore lists swallow legal calls like `@if()`. The exact-name stale-DB contract version also advances so older DBs with pre-fix canonical names degrade explicitly instead of silently returning false negatives. Added focused extractor and exact-name query regressions for normalized symbol names, qualified namespace/import lookups, normalized conversion-operator names, stale canonical-name DB detection, and normalized `call` / `instantiate` edges. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbContext.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Closes #367, #626, #627, #628.
- **C# parenthesized LINQ clause keywords no longer break query range-variable scope tracking (#564, #606, #607, #608, #609, #610, #612, #614, #615, #617, #621, #622, #623)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now recognizes legal parenthesized `select(` / `group(` / `orderby(` query clauses while keeping the top-level clause scanner anchored to real clause boundaries, so range-variable shadow scopes no longer survive past `select(`/`group(` or stay stuck in nested subqueries after `orderby(`. The follow-up guard now also rejects operator-adjacent and contextual-prefix local function calls such as `items.Count() > select(items)`, `< select(items)`, `! select(items)`, `await select(items)`, `throw select(items)` / `throw group(items)`, cast-close local calls like `(object)select(items)`, `(CustomType)select(items)`, `(customType)select(items)`, and parenthesized keyword-named value expressions like `(Select)` when they are not real casts, and split-line `select\n(items)` / `group\n(items)` while still allowing real clause boundaries after postfix null-forgiving, nullable-cast suffixes including array-rank forms, postfix increment, generic-close contexts like `Sink.Maybe(Status)! select(...)`, `value as Status? select(...)`, `value as Status[,]? select(...)`, `counter++ select(...)`, and `List<int> select(...)`, plus non-cast parenthesized `orderby` expressions such as `(flag ? left : right)`, `(left ?? right)`, and `(Demo.Status.Ready)` before `select(...)`. Added extractor and CLI `references --exact-name --json` / `inspect --exact-name --json` regressions for the reported repros, the operator/contextual follow-ups, postfix null-forgiving control, nullable/postfix controls, cast-close controls, generic-close control, split-line controls, simple-identifier and alias-cast controls, keyword-named value controls, qualified-member controls, and ternary/coalesce controls. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Closes #564, #606, #607, #608, #609, #610, #612, #614, #615, #617, #621, #622, #623.
- **Kotlin anonymous companion objects now default to `Companion` instead of emitting blank symbol names (#315)** — `SymbolExtractor` now normalizes unnamed `companion object { ... }` declarations to the Kotlin-default symbol name `Companion`, so `symbols`, `outline`, and `inspect` no longer surface empty-name rows and nested members such as `const val` / `fun` attach to `Companion` instead of an empty container. A defensive empty-name guard also blocks future blank symbol rows from entering the symbol list. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #315.
- **C# same-line header-line auto-properties now survive multiline type bodies (#580)** — `SymbolExtractor` now restarts the C# same-line pattern scan from just after a type header's opening `{` even when the enclosing `class` / `struct` / `interface` body closes on a later line. Hybrid shapes such as `public class Outer { public int P { get; }` + next-line `}` no longer stop after indexing only the outer type, so `symbols`, `definition`, `outline`, and `inspect` now keep the first member that shares the header line. Added focused extractor regressions for the `class`, `struct`, and `interface` variants and guards against phantom `property Outer`-style rows. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #580.
- **Brace-bodied last inner members in wrapped C# nested types no longer hide closing-brace-line outer siblings (#554)** — `SymbolExtractor` now uses the non-closing same-line restart helper when a compact C# type header (`Child { }`) is rejected as a false brace-property candidate, so the recovery path skips the intervening container-closing `}` and still reaches later outer siblings on the same line. Wrapped fixtures like `public partial class Child { } } public int Q { get; }` and `... } public partial class Sibling` now keep `Child` under `Wrapped` while preserving the outer property/class sibling for `symbols`, `definition`, and `outline`. Added focused extractor regressions for both the outer-property and outer-type sibling shapes. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #554.
- **C# switch-expression declaration-pattern guard parsing now also ignores `when` inside multiline block comments before `=>` (#597)** — `StructuralLineMasker` now blanks C# block-comment bodies and delimiters, so the switch-expression arm-preparation path no longer lets a multiline `/* ... when ... */` leak into `FindTopLevelCSharpWhenKeywordOffset()`. This closes the remaining false enum-member `call` regression from #583/#596 when a declaration-pattern arm keeps its real guard after a multiline comment, and adds extractor plus CLI `references --exact-name --json` coverage for the multiline repro. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/StructuralLineMasker.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Closes #597.
- **C# switch-expression declaration-pattern guard parsing now ignores `when` inside arm trivia before `=>` (#596)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now masks and prepares the switch-expression arm text before it tries to recover declaration-pattern designations, so block comments, line comments, and other non-code trivia can no longer trick the top-level `when` scan into truncating `Holder Status /* when comment */ => ...` as if a guard had started. This keeps the declaration-pattern value receiver alive and prevents the #583 false enum-member `call` edge from reappearing when inline comments contain `when`. Added extractor and CLI `inspect --exact-name --json` regressions for the comment-bearing repro while preserving the earlier guard/no-guard coverage. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Closes #596.
- **C# switch-expression declaration-pattern guards now keep pattern-variable shadow scopes alive (#583)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now recognizes switch-expression declaration patterns like `Holder Status when ... =>` before trimming any top-level `when` guard, so guard-side member access such as `Status.Ready` no longer leaks a false enum-member `call` edge while real `Demo.Status.Ready` hits in the arm body and fallback arms still survive. Added extractor regressions for both the `when`-guard repro and a no-guard control, plus CLI `references --exact-name --json` coverage for the reported case. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Closes #583.
- **C# query generic-comma scope tracking now keeps generic `as` null comparisons inside the selector scope (#591)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now treats `==` / `!=` immediately after a balanced generic close as valid terminal-selector continuations, so selectors such as `select Status as Dictionary<int, int> != null ? Status.Ready : 0` no longer drop the LINQ range-variable shadow scope at the `>` token. This prevents false enum-member `call` edges for generic `as` expressions compared against `null` while preserving real enum-member references that appear after the query. Added extractor and CLI `references --exact-name --json` / `inspect --exact-name --json` regressions for both `!= null` and `== null`, plus a control proving a real trailing enum reference still survives. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Closes #591.
- **C# query generic-comma scope tracking now keeps tuple-type generic arguments inside the selector scope (#590)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now lets balanced `>` tokens close terminal selector generic depth even when they occur inside tuple-type parentheses, so selectors such as `select Sink.Wrap<(int, List<int>)>(Status.Ready)` no longer drop the LINQ range-variable shadow scope before the trailing member access. This prevents false enum-member `call` edges when a terminal selector uses tuple types inside the generic argument list. Added extractor and CLI `references --exact-name --json` / `inspect --exact-name --json` regressions for the verified tuple-generic repro. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Closes #590.
- **C# query range-variable shadow scopes now stay alive through terminal selector generic commas (#581)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now keeps LINQ range-variable scopes open while `select` / `group` terminal expressions traverse balanced C# generic type-argument lists, so selectors such as `select Sink.Wrap<int, int>(Status.Ready)` no longer treat the comma inside `<int, int>` as the outer query boundary. This prevents false enum-member `call` edges for shadowed names like `Status.Ready` when `Status` is still the query range variable rather than the enum type. Added extractor regressions for multi-argument and single-argument generic selectors plus CLI `references --exact-name --json` / `inspect --exact-name --json` coverage for the reported repro. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Closes #581.
- **C# query generic-comma scope tracking no longer mistakes shift operators for generic delimiters (#586)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now rejects operator-shaped `<` tokens such as `<<` / `<=` before arming terminal query-selector generic depth, so LINQ selectors like `select (Status.Ready << 1) >> (1 + Status.Ready)` keep the range-variable shadow scope alive through the full expression instead of terminating at the `>>`. This prevents the #581 fix from reintroducing false enum-member `call` edges inside the same selector while still preserving real enum references that appear after the query. Added extractor and CLI `references --exact-name --json` / `inspect --exact-name --json` regressions for the verified shift-expression repro. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Closes #586.
- **C# query generic-comma scope tracking now keeps type-pattern generic designations inside the selector scope (#587)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now treats an identifier that follows a balanced generic close as a valid selector continuation, so terminal patterns like `Status is Dictionary<int, int> dict ? Status.Ready : 0` no longer drop the LINQ range-variable shadow scope at `> dict`. This prevents false enum-member `call` edges when a terminal selector uses a generic type pattern with a designation variable after the `>` token. Added extractor and CLI `references --exact-name --json` / `inspect --exact-name --json` regressions for the verified type-pattern repro. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Closes #587.
- **C# query generic-comma scope tracking now keeps designation-free generic type patterns inside the selector scope (#588)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now treats `?` after a balanced generic close as a valid selector continuation, so terminal patterns like `Status is Dictionary<int, int> ? Status.Ready : 0` no longer drop the LINQ range-variable shadow scope at the `>` token. This prevents false enum-member `call` edges when a terminal selector uses a generic type pattern without a designation variable. Added extractor and CLI `references --exact-name --json` / `inspect --exact-name --json` regressions for the verified no-designation type-pattern repro. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Closes #588.
- **Compact same-line C# type bodies now attribute enum-member references to the innermost member container (#546)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now resolves C# same-line nested containers with column-aware signature spans instead of relying only on body-line width ordering, so compact fixtures like `class C { int N => 0; void M() { ... Color.Red; } }` no longer collapse `Red` onto `class C` just because the class, property, and method all share one physical line. `references`, `callers`, and `inspect` now report the inline method/property container that actually owns the call site, while existing multi-line control cases keep their prior behavior. Added focused extractor and CLI regressions covering same-line method attribution, same-line property attribution, and the multiline control case. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Closes #546.
- **Wrapped C# nested-type members, family keys, and closing-brace-line siblings now stay on the canonical container path after the #525 header fix (#535, #541, #545, #549, #552, #558, #571)** — `SymbolExtractor.AssignContainers` now keeps same-line nested type containers available for later inner members even when earlier same-line siblings belong to the outer type, and resolves each symbol's effective container path from the currently open container stack instead of permanently evicting non-matching same-line nested types. `BuildSelfFamilyKey` now preserves that root-to-leaf container order as well, so nested partial types no longer flip persisted family keys like `Host.ReviewFixtures.Wrapped`. The line-level C# scan also skips intervening top-level `}` tokens when restarting toward the next same-line statement boundary, so wrapped nested types can close on `} public int Q { get; }` without silently dropping the later outer sibling, and container assignment now reconstructs the matching closing-brace column on that raw end line so `public int P { get; } } public int Q { get; }` keeps `P` inside `Wrapped` while attaching `Q` to `Host`. Duplicate suppression now also allows as many exact same-line rows as the raw line truly contains, so `public partial class Child { } } public partial class Child { }` keeps both the inner and outer `Child` declarations instead of collapsing the later sibling before container assignment runs. Same-line occurrence tracking also ignores C# string/comment lookalikes when mapping a declaration to its raw-line occurrence, and C# match-line construction now keeps multiline block-comment state from the raw source when scanning same-line declarations, so markers like `\"public partial class Child { }\"` or `/* ... */ public partial class Child { }` no longer shift or duplicate the real `Child` siblings onto the wrong occurrence slot. This preserves the #525 wrapped-header signature fix while restoring correct inner-container assignment for wrapped fixtures, keeping hotspot-family keys aligned with `container_qualified_name`, and preserving both inner and outer siblings on the closing-brace line without leaking comment-close phantom rows. Added focused regression coverage for the exact issue fixture, a nested partial-type family-key fixture, a closing-brace-line outer-sibling fixture, the compact closing-line `P ... } Q` fixture, the duplicate-signature `Child ... } Child` fixture, the string-literal lookalike fixture, and the multiline comment-close fixture. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #535, #541, #545, #549, #552, #558, #571.
- **Wrapped closing-brace-line container recovery now carries unmatched inner brace depth across prior lines (#575)** — `FindCSharpClosingBraceColumnOnContainerEndLine` previously carried only the lexical comment/string state into the raw end-line scan and then restarted brace matching from a hard-coded depth of `1`. In fixtures such as `public void M() { } public int P { get; } } public int Q { get; }`, that made the first `}` on the closing line look like the wrapped type's close even when it only closed an inner method body, so `P` was incorrectly attached to `Host` instead of `Wrapped`. The helper now accumulates sanitized `{` / `}` depth from `BodyStartLine` through the line before `EndLine` and starts the final-line scan from that carried depth, so later inner members stay inside the wrapped type until the real container-closing brace is reached. Added a focused regression test that keeps `P` under `Wrapped` while leaving `Q` under `Host`. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #575.
- **Wrapped C# nested same-line occurrence recovery now also respects carried multiline block-comment state (#567)** — The #558 fix sanitized string/comment lookalikes per raw line, but it still re-lexed the occurrence-search line with a fresh `CSharpLexState`, so a declaration-shaped lookalike inside a `/* ... */` block comment carried from the previous physical line could still consume the first occurrence slot. In fixtures such as `/*\npublic partial class Child { } */ public partial class Child { } } public partial class Child { }`, that stale start state shifted the later real sibling onto the wrong start column and attached both `Child` symbols to the inner `Wrapped` type instead of splitting them between `Wrapped` and `Host`. `Extract` now precomputes per-line C# start states and threads them through `AssignContainers`, so same-line start-column recovery and same-line closing-brace reconstruction sanitize each raw line with the correct carried lexer state instead of assuming `Code` mode at column 0. Added a focused regression test that locks the multiline-comment repro and asserts the two `Child` symbols still land on `Wrapped` and `Host` respectively. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #567.
- **C# declaration-pattern and dotted receiver shadowing no longer leak phantom enum-member edges (#510, #511, #555, #561, #565, #569, #570, #574)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now treats declaration-pattern variables as scoped value receivers across wrapped `if` / `while` headers, conditional-expression true branches, `switch` case arms, switch-expression recursive/property pattern arms such as `Holder { Ready: > 0 } Status => ...`, single-line and multi-line recursive/property patterns such as `Holder { Ready: > 0 } Status`, dotted qualifiers whose first segment is a value receiver, and declaration-pattern variables introduced inside plain or `static` lambdas nested in conditional headers. This stops false `call` edges from `Status.Ready` after declaration-pattern binding, from receiver chains like `RealNs.Status.Ready` when `RealNs` is a parameter/property rather than a namespace/type qualifier, and from lambda-contained declaration patterns incorrectly hiding or inventing enum-member edges in surrounding scopes. Added extractor and CLI regressions covering same-line and multi-line declaration-pattern cases, lambda-contained declaration patterns, `switch`/`case`, switch expressions, conditional expressions, recursive/property patterns, `static` lambdas, and dotted receiver chains while preserving real enum-member references later in the same member. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Closes #510, #511, #555, #561, #565, #569, #570, #574.
- **C# query range-variable shadow scope no longer ends at `orderby` commas (#532)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now uses a query-aware C# scope terminator for LINQ range variables instead of the generic statement terminator, so top-level commas inside `orderby a, b` remain inside the same query expression until the real outer boundary. The query-clause scanner now only lets the most recent real outer clause control comma termination, which prevents nested subqueries inside `let` expressions from arming `terminalClauseSeen` early and then leaking later `select Status.Ready` enum-member false positives when the outer query reaches `orderby items.Count(), nested.Count()`. Terminal `group ... by ...` clauses now also stay armed across the `by` keyword so query-in-argument cases still stop at the real comma before a later enum expression, and terminal `select` expressions no longer let simple names such as `descending` masquerade as `orderby` modifiers that clear the comma boundary. Query-clause keywords now also require a legal clause suffix, so lower-case identifiers such as `select from(items)` inside the selected expression are no longer misread as fresh LINQ clauses that suppress the real comma boundary before a later `Status.Ready`. This also preserves the earlier fixes for `descending` / `ascending` multi-key orderings, member names such as `item.select` / `item . select`, escaped identifiers such as `@select`, and `orderby` keys that use anonymous-type / object-initializer braces before a later comma. Added extractor and CLI regressions covering plain/directional comma orderings, nested `let ... from ... select ...` continuations, `group ... by ...` query-in-argument tails, lower-case query-keyword identifiers inside selected expressions, terminal `select descending, ...` tails, member-name spacing, and initializer-key cases through `references --exact-name --json` and `inspect --exact-name --json`. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Closes #532.
- **C# `global::Enum.Member` references now keep true global-escape semantics across shadowing and alias collisions (#531, #547)** — `ReferenceExtractor` now preserves an explicit leading `global::` qualifier through C# qualified-access parsing, skips both the value-receiver shadow filter and `using`-alias rewriting for that escape form, and therefore keeps `global::Color.Red` / `global::Demo.Color.Red` bound to the real global namespace even when the surrounding scope contains a same-named property, local, or alias. This also stops compile-invalid alias-only forms such as `global::Color.Red` from being rewritten into real enum-member edges via alias resolution. Added extractor and CLI regressions covering `references` and `inspect` for property-shadow, conflicting-alias, and alias-only-invalid cases. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Closes #531, #547.
- **C# same-line generic brace-bodied members now restart sibling scans in the correct column domain (#533)** — `SymbolExtractor` now translates single-line C# brace-body end columns between raw-source and collapsed match-line coordinates before slicing signatures or restarting same-line sibling scans, so fixtures like `void M<T1,           T2>() { }int P { get; }event System.Action<int,           string> E;` keep the method signature bounded at its own body and still expose later property/event siblings. Added regression coverage in `SymbolExtractorTests`. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #533.
- **C# same-line nested interface properties now stay attached to the inner interface container (#529)** — `SymbolExtractor` now records same-line symbol start columns and uses them to preserve source order during `AssignContainers`, so longer earlier siblings no longer reorder compact nested-interface members by signature length alone. This keeps fixtures like `public interface I2 { void M<T1, T2>(); event System.Action<int, string> E; interface J { int P { get; } } }` from popping `J` off the container stack before `P` is processed, and `property P` now retains `container_name = "J"` instead of falling back to outer `I2`. Added extractor regression coverage and verified the fix with the local `cdidx.dll` repro. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Models/SymbolRecord.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #529.
- **Windows CI now pins `TMP`/`TEMP` to `RUNNER_TEMP` before restore/build/test so temp-heavy fixtures avoid the slower default profile temp path (#527)** — The Windows `Build and Test` and `Release` workflows already carried Defender exclusions from #394, but the test suite still routes most temp projects/DBs through `Path.GetTempPath()`, which kept landing under the default user-profile temp root on hosted runners. Both workflows now create a dedicated `RUNNER_TEMP\cdidx-temp` directory, export it through `GITHUB_ENV` before the Defender-exclusion step, and log the effective `TMP`/`TEMP` plus `.NET` `Path.GetTempPath()` resolution. This keeps the temp-heavy test churn on the same CI-owned volume as the workspace and ensures the later Defender verification step checks the actual effective temp root rather than stale defaults. Affected: `.github/workflows/dotnet.yml`, `.github/workflows/release.yml`. Closes #527, #530.
- **Same-line C# type bodies now preserve mixed-member siblings across property/event shapes (#472)** — `SymbolExtractor` now rejects outer-type brace-property false positives by restarting from the first member inside the same-line type body instead of the regex tail, defers C# function/property same-line advancement when the current statement start is itself a property/event/delegate declaration, and restarts the full pattern list after same-line property/event/delegate declarations reach a later sibling so earlier regex rows can still see that sibling. Exact duplicate symbol rows are also suppressed when same-line restart paths revisit the same declaration, and C# same-line `event` / `delegate` signatures are now clamped at the declaration terminator instead of absorbing the enclosing type's closing brace. Compact fixtures such as `public class C { public int A => 1; public int P { get; set; } }`, `public class C { public int P { get; set; } public void M() { } }`, `public class C { public int P { get; set; } public event System.EventHandler E; }`, and `public class C { public event System.EventHandler E; public void M() { } }` now emit the expected symbols exactly once. Added regression coverage in `SymbolExtractorTests`. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #472.
- **C# same-line mixed member streams no longer drop earlier siblings behind later pattern families (#473)** — `SymbolExtractor` now treats compact same-line C# type-body members as a statement stream instead of letting earlier pattern families scan past sibling boundaries, so shapes like `event E; public int P { get; set; }`, `void M(); int P { get; }`, accessor-event forms like `event E { add {} remove {} } public int P { get; set; }`, and reverse-order `property + event` lines all emit each member with the correct same-line signature boundary. Added regression coverage for class/struct/interface mixed-kind fixtures, including accessor-event follow-up coverage from #520. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #473, #520.
- **C# same-line generic compact-member chains now keep both later brace-bodied signatures and compact-sibling restarts aligned (#525)** — `SymbolExtractor` now reuses the raw-column start position for same-line C# brace-body and wrapped-header signature slicing whenever the regex matched against the collapsed per-line generic buffer, so later siblings after `void M<T1, T2>(); event Action<int, string> E; ...` no longer keep phantom leading `;` / `{` in property or nested-interface signatures. The same fix set now also translates raw same-line brace endpoints back into the collapsed match-line column domain before restarting compact-sibling scans, so dense shapes like `void M<T1,           T2>() { }int P { get; }event ...` no longer drop `P` / `E` or absorb them into `M`'s signature. Added focused regression coverage for the direct property, nested-interface, and no-space compact-chain shapes. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #525, #533.
- **C# enum-member shadowing now uses offset-scoped lifetimes for same-line lambdas, query expressions, and brace-less embedded statements (#371)** — `ReferenceExtractor` no longer drops real enum-member references merely because a same-line lambda parameter, `foreach` variable, or LINQ range variable reuses the same identifier later on the line. Shadow scopes are now tracked by exact start/end offsets instead of line-wide suppression, parenthesized lambdas start at the actual `(` position, query range variables stop at the enclosing subexpression boundary, and brace-less `if/else` embedded statements are parsed as a single scope-bearing unit so later tails are not cut off. Added extractor and CLI regressions covering same-line lambda/foreach tails, query-in-argument cases, parenthesized lambda prefixes, and brace-less `if/else` forms. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Closes #371.

### [1.13.0] - 2026-04-21

#### Changed
- **Distributed installs now keep persistent stderr breadcrumbs with 30-day retention** — `ProgramRunner` now enables `GlobalToolLog` for distributed/non-development executions and mirrors terminal stderr plus minimal lifecycle metadata into a per-user daily file named `stderr-YYYYMMDD.log`. Repository-local development runs from `src/CodeIndex/bin/...` and `tests/.../bin/...` stay excluded by default so normal build/test cycles do not accumulate persistent logs. Default log roots are `%LOCALAPPDATA%\cdidx\logs\` on Windows, `~/Library/Logs/cdidx/` on macOS, and `$XDG_STATE_HOME/cdidx/logs/` (or `~/.local/state/cdidx/logs/`) on Linux. The log directory is pruned to the newest 30 daily files on startup, `CDIDX_DISABLE_PERSISTENT_LOG=1` disables the feature, and `CDIDX_GLOBAL_TOOL_LOG_DIR` remains available for test/packaging overrides. Added regression coverage for stderr mirroring, 30-file pruning, and explicit opt-out, and documented the behavior in the user and developer guides. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Cli/GlobalToolLog.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Cli/ProgramRunner.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ProgramRunnerTests.cs`, `README.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`.
- **Interactive indexing/output no longer goes visually silent after `Indexing...`** — `IndexCommandRunner` now keeps a live `Indexing...` spinner active on interactive terminals during full-scan indexing work instead of stopping it before the first file and leaving the user with a fixed line until the next 50-file progress redraw or warning. Scoped update runs now also keep a live `Updating...` spinner under the same interactive-only contract, pausing only long enough to print `[WARN]`, `[OK]`, `[SKIP]`, `[DEL]`, `[ERR]`, or progress-bar output cleanly and then resuming immediately. Redirected stdout behavior is intentionally unchanged in shape: stdout still gets a single `Indexing...` banner plus line-based progress, while warnings stay on stderr, so logs/pipes do not collect spinner-frame noise. README examples now document both the interactive and redirected-output behavior, and `SELF_IMPROVEMENT.md` now explicitly treats visible liveness for long-running human-facing CLI flows as a regression bar. Added regression coverage ensuring redirected full-scan output prints `Indexing...` exactly once and redirected verbose update output does not duplicate the `Updating ... file(s)...` banner. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Cli/IndexCommandRunner.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/IndexCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `README.md`, `SELF_IMPROVEMENT.md`.

#### Fixed
- **JavaScript prototype / namespace assignments and named function expressions index again (#227)** — `SymbolExtractor` now captures qualified assignments such as `Vehicle.prototype.start = function () {}`, `Foo.Bar = class {}`, and `Vehicle.VERSION = "1.0"`, named function expressions such as `var add = function add() {}`, and object-literal function / arrow property values such as `onSubmit: function submitHandler() {}` / `onClose: () => {}`. Added regressions for qualified class/function/property assignments, object-literal property values, and nested class-member containers, pinning that `definition` / `symbols` / `outline` keep the outer anchor instead of falling to floating inner names. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Fixes #227.
- **Plain C# interpolated strings no longer hide later helpers from symbol extraction (#882)** — `SymbolExtractor` now avoids desynchronizing the C# lexer when plain `$"..."` interpolation holes contain nested string literals, so later helpers in the same file remain visible in `symbols` and `definition`. Added a regression for the DbReader-style SQL fixture. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #882.
- **`callers` human output now surfaces grouped `reference_kind` tags without changing JSON (#383)** — `RunCallers` now prints each human-readable caller row with a leading grouped `reference_kind` tag so terminal users can distinguish `call`, `instantiate`, and `subscribe` edges without rerunning the command with `--json`. The read path now carries an internal grouped caller kind while keeping the serialized `CallerResult` JSON shape unchanged, and regression coverage locks a ctor-chain-plus-`new` fixture in `QueryCommandRunnerTests`. Documentation was updated in both README and DEVELOPER_GUIDE. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Cli/QueryCommandRunner.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Models/QueryResults.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `README.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #383.
- **C# explicit-interface extraction no longer invents phantom methods from qualified `new Namespace.Type()` expressions (#362)** — The C# explicit-interface method regex now excludes `new` in the return-type position, so standalone constructor expressions such as `new System.Text.StringBuilder()` and `new System.Net.Http.HttpClient()` are no longer indexed as fake `function StringBuilder` / `function HttpClient` rows. Added extractor regression coverage that keeps real explicit-interface members such as `void IDisposable.Dispose()` working while suppressing the phantom constructor-name rows. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #362.
- **C# generic-over-tuple return types no longer disappear from symbol extraction (#344, #484)** — `SymbolExtractor` now treats embedded tuple groups inside C# type tokens as real return-type segments only when the parenthesized group is tuple-shaped (contains a comma at the outer tuple level), which lets ordinary methods, interface declarations, and explicit-interface implementations capture shapes such as `Task<(int, string)>`, `Dictionary<string, (int x, int y)>`, `IEnumerable<(string Key, int Value)>`, `List<(int, int)> IFoo.GetList()`, `Task<((int A, int B), string Name)>`, and `Task<(((int A, int B), int C), string Name)>` without reintroducing the primary-ctor / collection-expression false positives guarded by the existing `ReferenceExtractor` regression suite. Added regression coverage for the new generic-over-tuple cases plus the nearby primary-ctor and collection-expression cases that would otherwise misfire if non-tuple `(...)` groups were accepted too broadly. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #344, #484.
- **Same-line C# static constructors now surface before later sibling properties can short-circuit symbol extraction** — The dedicated C# static-constructor regex now runs before the brace/expression-bodied property rows, so compact one-line type bodies such as `public class E { static E() { } public int R { get; set; } }` emit both the static constructor and the later property instead of losing the ctor after the property row claims the later statement and stops the pattern scan. Added regression coverage that locks both instance/static same-line constructors and their later sibling auto-properties in one fixture. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #478.
- **DEVELOPER_GUIDE now keeps a single authoritative C# support row in both language tables (#483)** — Removed the duplicated older C# row from the English and Japanese "Supported symbol kinds by language" tables so each section keeps the newer constructor-lookahead wording in one canonical entry instead of two conflicting rows. Affected: `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`. Closes #483.
- **Added explicit regression coverage for the existing C# complex enum-value fix (#357)** — Current `main` already indexes C# enum members whose assigned values start with member access, casts, parenthesized expressions, and framework constant lookups such as `K.Foo`, `(int)1.5`, `(1 + 2)`, `(int)'A'`, `System.Int32.MaxValue`, and `[Flags]` aliases like `Execute = K.Foo`, but the open issue's exact `E1` / `Permissions` dogfood fixture was not pinned end-to-end. Added an extractor-level regression that keeps those value shapes under `SymbolExtractorTests`, plus `QueryCommandRunner` integration coverage that indexes the same file and asserts both `symbols --json --exact-name` and `definition --json --exact-name` still resolve the affected members under the correct owning enum. Affected: `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Closes #357.
- **Added explicit regression coverage for the existing C# object-initializer phantom-enum fix (#374)** — Current `main` already suppresses object-initializer assignments such as `Age = 30,` and `Priority = 2` from reappearing as phantom C# enum members, but the open issue's exact dogfood fixture was not pinned end-to-end. Added an extractor-level regression that uses the full issue repro with both multiline and inline `new Person { ... }` initializers, plus a `QueryCommandRunner` integration test that round-trips the indexed file through `symbols --json --exact-name` and asserts `Age` / `Priority` resolve only to the real `Person` properties. This keeps issue #374 closable without relying on an older, narrower unit fixture alone. Affected: `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Closes #374.
- **C# interface and struct members now keep their immediate type container instead of falling back to the enclosing namespace** — `AssignContainers` now treats `interface` and `struct` symbols as container-stack candidates, so members such as interface events and struct properties/events inherit `container_kind` / `container_name` / `container_qualified_name` from the enclosing type rather than the surrounding namespace. Added regression coverage for the #466 interface-event repro and the same-root-cause struct-member case tracked in #468. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #466.
- **C# interface events now stay attached to their interface container in symbol output** — `AssignContainers` now treats `interface` and `struct` as real symbol containers instead of only `class` / `namespace` / `enum`, so members declared inside them inherit the innermost enclosing type instead of falling back to the surrounding namespace. This fixes the concrete `#334` dogfood fixture where `IBus.Regular`, `IBus.StaticAbs`, and `IBus.StaticVirt` were emitted as namespace-level events in `symbols`, `outline`, and `inspect`, and adds regression coverage that locks both the modifier acceptance and the interface container attribution in place. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #466.
- **Same-line C# type bodies now keep brace-body auto-properties instead of dropping them behind false outer-type matches** — `SymbolExtractor` now treats rejected C# brace-property false positives as same-line scan misses instead of terminal breaks, so lines like `public class C { public int P { get; set; } }`, `public struct S { public int Q { get; set; } }`, and `public interface I { int R { get; } }` advance past the outer type header and still emit the nested property. Container assignment now also recognizes `struct` and `interface` as same-line containers, so the recovered properties attach to `S` / `I` rather than falling back to the namespace. Added regression coverage for same-line class/struct/interface auto-properties plus same-line method/event controls. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`. Closes #470.
- **`status --json` now exposes hotspot-family trust signals before `hotspots` runs (#460)** — `StatusResult` now carries `hotspot_family_ready` plus `hotspot_family_degraded_reason`, `DbReader.GetStatus()` populates those fields from the existing hotspot-family readiness signal, and the one-line status summary now marks the index as degraded when duplicate-name hotspot families are not yet authoritative. To keep the operational guidance consistent, `cdidx index` JSON/human readiness output and degraded-readiness warnings now surface the same hotspot-family trust bit as well. Added CLI and MCP regression coverage so automation can discover degraded hotspot-family metadata up front instead of probing `hotspots --json` first. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Cli/IndexCommandRunner.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Cli/QueryCommandRunner.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbReader.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Database/DbWriter.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Models/QueryResults.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/IndexCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/McpServerTests.cs`, `README.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`, `CLAUDE.md`. Closes #460.
- **Nested generic C#/Java call sites no longer disappear from the reference index** — `ReferenceExtractor` now keeps the existing regex fast path for flat calls, then runs a depth-aware fallback scanner for nested-generic tails that end in `>>(` so outer constructor/method targets such as `new Dictionary<string, List<int>>()`, `new ArrayList<HashMap<String, Integer>>()`, and `Helper.DoWork<List<int>>()` still emit `instantiate` / `call` rows instead of silently dropping the entire call site. Added C# and Java regression coverage and documented the extraction rule in the developer docs. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/ReferenceExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/ReferenceExtractorTests.cs`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`, `CLAUDE.md`. Closes #263.
- **C# symbol extraction no longer goes super-linear on `InstallScriptTests.cs`-style raw-string fixtures without truncating long field signatures** — `BuildCSharpPropertyMatchLine` now uses a bounded confirmation phase only for suspicious multiline field/property candidates, then switches to a linear continuation scan once the candidate is confirmed as a real declaration (`name = ...` or a named member header before `{`). That stops raw-string + heredoc bodies from triggering repeated whole-tail re-normalization across the rest of a large file, while still preserving full `symbols` / `definition` / `outline` signatures for wrapped raw-string fields, same-line `const` raw-string fields, and long object/collection initializers well past the 16-line confirmation budget. Added regression tests for the real `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/InstallScriptTests.cs` fixture, extractor-level >16-line raw-string/object-initializer signatures, and CLI-level `symbols` / `outline` round-tripping of long raw-string fields. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Indexer/SymbolExtractor.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/SymbolExtractorTests.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/QueryCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Closes #447.
- **Full `cdidx index <projectPath>` runs now recover hotspot-family trust after metadata-only loss without forcing `--rebuild`** — Full-scan reuse is now gated per hotspot-family marker language instead of by a single global `skipped == 0` check. If the stored C#/VB/F# hotspot-family metadata is missing or stale, unchanged files in that language are reindexed during the next normal full scan so the authoritative `family_key` / `container_qualified_name` rows are regenerated and `hotspot_family_ready` is restamped. Unchanged files in other languages may still be reused, and already-current hotspot-family languages keep their trust even when unrelated marker files change. Added regression coverage for the `#459` shape where only the C# hotspot-family metadata is cleared and the next `cdidx index .` run must restore both the metadata and `hotspots --json` readiness on a partially skipped scan. Affected: `src/CodeIndex/Cli/IndexCommandRunner.cs`, `src/CodeIndex/Mcp/McpToolHandlers.cs`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/IndexCommandRunnerTests.cs`. Closes #459.

### [1.12.0] - 2026-04-19

#### Changed
- **Installer cloud documentation expanded for Claude Code *and* Codex cloud harnesses** — `README.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`, and `CLOUD_BOOTSTRAP_PROMPT.md` now explicitly document that the published `install.sh` flow is intended for cloud AI coding containers beyond Claude Code (including Codex-style harnesses), with mirrored bilingual guidance for restricted-egress setups, mirror/proxy env vars, and built-in local self-test invocation. The new `--self-test-local-mirror` path now defaults to an isolated temporary install directory unless a **non-empty** `CDIDX_INSTALL_DIR` is explicitly set, so a mock self-test cannot silently overwrite an existing real `~/.local/bin/cdidx` even when `CDIDX_INSTALL_DIR=""` is present in the environment. It also validates local mirror startup on `127.0.0.1` before download begins, forces loopback traffic to bypass outbound proxies via `NO_PROXY` / `no_proxy` plus `curl --noproxy`, and reports mirror-specific startup failures with log tail output instead of misclassifying them as generic external network/proxy errors. Permission-denied loopback bind failures are now differentiated from "port already in use" failures, and the docs now call out the mirror/proxy env vars, the loopback-listen prerequisite, and `CDIDX_LOCAL_MIRROR_PORT`. Added regression coverage for isolated self-test installs, explicit install-dir override behavior, empty-string install-dir handling, configured mirror override URLs, loopback proxy bypass, custom-port wiring, permission-denied bind failures, and generic local-mirror startup diagnostics. Affected: `README.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`, `CLOUD_BOOTSTRAP_PROMPT.md`, `install.sh`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/InstallScriptTests.cs`.
- **Installer 403 troubleshooting now distinguishes API lookup failures from tunnel-policy denies** — `install.sh` now gives concrete next steps for HTTP 403 during latest-release lookup and asset download (explicit version pinning, mirror API override, and upstream proxy allow-list guidance), and `CLOUD_BOOTSTRAP_PROMPT.md` now explicitly states that `CONNECT tunnel failed, response 403` across every candidate host is an egress-policy deny that route substitution alone cannot fix. Affected: `install.sh`, `CLOUD_BOOTSTRAP_PROMPT.md`.
- **Installer self-test refuses to overwrite a real install even when `CDIDX_INSTALL_DIR` is pre-exported** — `install.sh --self-test-local-mirror` previously honored an explicit non-empty `CDIDX_INSTALL_DIR` as-is, so users who pre-export `CDIDX_INSTALL_DIR=$HOME/.local/bin` for normal installs could silently replace their real `cdidx` binary with the mock payload (which only responds to `--version`). The self-test now detects risky install directories — well-known system paths (`/usr/local/bin`, `/usr/bin`, `/opt/homebrew/bin`, `/opt/local/bin`), `$HOME/.local/bin`, and any directory already containing a `cdidx` executable — and aborts with a clear message telling the operator to either unset `CDIDX_INSTALL_DIR` so the self-test runs in an isolated temp dir, or pass the new `--self-test-allow-overwrite` flag if they really want to inspect the mock layout in place. The escape hatch is intentionally CLI-only: `SELF_TEST_ALLOW_OVERWRITE=1` from the caller's environment is no longer inherited, so a stale env var in the user's shell or CI cannot silently reintroduce the bypass. Path comparison also strips trailing slashes before matching the well-known-path list, so `/usr/local/bin/` and `$HOME/.local/bin/` are treated identically to the no-slash forms instead of slipping past exact-string equality. `CLOUD_BOOTSTRAP_PROMPT.md` and `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md` document both the abort behavior and the escape hatch. Added regression coverage for the home-local-bin abort, the pre-existing-cdidx abort, a pre-exported `SELF_TEST_ALLOW_OVERWRITE=1` env var that must NOT bypass the guard, trailing-slash variants of every well-known system path and `$HOME/.local/bin/`, the CLI (`--self-test-allow-overwrite`) override, and a fresh custom dir that should pass through without the guard. Affected: `install.sh`, `CLOUD_BOOTSTRAP_PROMPT.md`, `DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md`, `tests/CodeIndex.Tests/InstallScriptTests.cs`.

#### Added
